faculty.name: Harish Ravichandar
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Researches scalable approaches for multi-agent and human-robot interaction, developing machine learning methods for coordinated multi-robot and human-robot teams. Works on foundation models for robotics, task and motion planning, and interactive decision-making.

faculty.name: Danfei Xu
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Researches robot learning at the intersection of computer vision, machine learning, and human-robot interaction. Develops methods for robots to learn from human demonstrations, understand human intent, and adapt their behavior in interactive manipulation tasks.

faculty.name: James Hays
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Researches computer vision with applications to robotics and autonomous systems, focusing on scene understanding, image synthesis and manipulation detection, and learning visual representations from large-scale data for robotics perception tasks.

faculty.name: Irfan Essa
faculty.title: Distinguished Professor
faculty.summary: Researches analysis, interpretation, and synthesis of human activities using time-varying data streams. Works on video understanding, computational photography, and machine perception with applications to robotics, human activity recognition, and autonomous systems.

faculty.name: Samuel Coogan
faculty.title: Demetrius T. Paris Junior Professor and Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Researches control and coordination of autonomous multi-agent systems, with applications to traffic networks, robotic teams, and cyber-physical systems. Develops formal methods and control algorithms for safe and efficient autonomous operation in complex environments.

faculty.name: Patricio Vela
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Researches computer vision integrated with robot control, developing visual servoing techniques and perception-driven autonomy for robotic systems. Works on vision-based navigation, object tracking, and autonomous manipulation using visual feedback.

faculty.name: Azadeh Ansari
faculty.title: Sutterfield Family Early Career Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Researches micro-scale robotic systems and MEMS devices, developing novel microfabricated sensors and actuators for autonomous micro-robots. Designs and builds insect-scale robotic platforms with applications in environmental monitoring and inspection.

faculty.name: Panagiotis Tsiotras
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Directs the Dynamics and Control Systems Laboratory, researching control and autonomy for aerial and space robotics. Develops algorithms for UAV guidance, navigation, control, and autonomous decision-making for aerospace vehicles and planetary rovers.

faculty.name: Karen Feigh
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Researches the intersection of humans and autonomous systems, developing cognitive engineering approaches for designing effective human-automation interaction. Works on decision support systems for aviation, spacecraft operations, and complex autonomous systems.

faculty.name: Evangelos Theodorou
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Directs the Autonomous Control and Decision Systems Lab, researching stochastic optimal control and reinforcement learning for autonomous systems. Develops information-theoretic approaches to control with applications to autonomous driving, agile aerial robotics, and robot manipulation.

faculty.name: Kyriakos Vamvoudakis
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Researches learning-based control and decision-making for autonomous and multi-agent robotic systems. Develops reinforcement learning and game-theoretic approaches for adaptive and resilient control of cyber-physical systems and robotic teams.

faculty.name: Glen Chou
faculty.title: Assistant Professor (School of Cybersecurity and Privacy and Aerospace Engineering)
faculty.summary: Researches safe and trustworthy robot control, developing formal verification methods and learning-based planning algorithms that provide safety guarantees for autonomous robots. Works on scalable approaches for verified control synthesis and safe human-robot interaction.

faculty.name: Aaron Young
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Directs the EPIC (Exoskeleton and Prosthetic Intelligent Controls) Lab, researching wearable robotic systems for human augmentation and rehabilitation. Develops intent recognition, control strategies, and mechatronic design for powered prostheses and exoskeletons.

faculty.name: Ye Zhao
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Directs the Laboratory for Intelligent Decision and Autonomous Robots (LIDAR), researching planning and control for legged robots. Develops whole-body motion planning and model predictive control algorithms for bipedal and quadrupedal locomotion in challenging environments.

faculty.name: Jonathan Rogers
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Researches nonlinear control theory and its application to robotic systems, including autonomous ground and aerial vehicles. Works on dynamics, estimation, and mechatronic design for high-performance robotic platforms.

faculty.name: Jaydev Desai
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Directs the Robotics and Mechatronics Lab (RoboMed), researching surgical and interventional robotics. Develops MRI-compatible robotic systems, haptic feedback for robot-assisted surgery, and micro-robotic platforms for minimally invasive medical procedures.

faculty.name: Jun Ueda
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Researches novel actuation and mechatronic systems for robotics, including cellular actuators inspired by biological muscle. Develops precision motion systems and robotic devices for human-robot interaction and rehabilitation applications.

faculty.name: Lena Ting
faculty.title: McCamish Foundation Distinguished Chair Professor
faculty.summary: Researches neuromechanics of posture and locomotion, studying how the nervous system controls movement and balance. Work informs the design of bio-inspired robotic control, rehabilitation robotics, and wearable assistive devices.

faculty.name: Isaac S. Kohane
faculty.title: Professor and Chair of Biomedical Informatics, Harvard Medical School
faculty.summary: Founding Chair of DBMI at Harvard Medical School. Pioneer in biomedical informatics and clinical data science. Creator of the i2b2 platform. Leads the Undiagnosed Diseases Network Coordinating Center. Work on neuropsychiatric disease genomics and research data-sharing infrastructure.

faculty.name: Peter J. Park
faculty.title: Professor of Biomedical Informatics, Harvard Medical School
faculty.summary: Leading researcher in cancer genomics and mutational processes. Work on computational methods for genome and epigenome analysis, somatic mutations in cancer and normal cells. Director of the Park Lab for computational biology.

faculty.name: Tianxi Cai
faculty.title: Professor of Biomedical Informatics, Harvard Medical School; John Rock Professor of Population and Translational Data Sciences, Harvard Chan School
faculty.summary: Leading biostatistician developing statistical and machine learning methods for electronic health record data mining. Work on predictive modeling, real-world evidence generation, and personalized medicine. Co-director of CELEHS (Cai Lab).

faculty.name: Shamil R. Sunyaev
faculty.title: Professor of Biomedical Informatics, Harvard Medical School
faculty.summary: Leading researcher in population genetic variation and evolutionary genomics. Work on analyzing patterns of sequence divergence to understand disease risk, human evolution, and the functional impact of genetic variants.

faculty.name: Heng Li
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Biomedical Informatics, Harvard Medical School; Associate Professor of Data Science, Dana-Farber Cancer Institute
faculty.summary: Creator of widely-used bioinformatics tools including BWA, SAMtools, minimap2, and hifiasm, collectively cited over 100,000 times. Leading researcher in sequence alignment algorithms, genome assembly, and computational methods for biological problems.

faculty.name: Nils Gehlenborg
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Biomedical Informatics, Harvard Medical School
faculty.summary: Leading researcher in computational visualization techniques for biomedical data. Director of the HIDIVE Lab. Co-founder of BioVis and VIZBI. Work on visualizing heterogeneous data from large-scale cancer genomics studies (TCGA), 3D genome conformation, and EHR data.

faculty.name: Chirag Patel
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Biomedical Informatics, Harvard Medical School
faculty.summary: Leading researcher in computational and bioinformatics methods for studying gene-environment interactions. Work on the exposome, integrating EHR data to understand complex disease etiology, and machine learning approaches to environmental health.

faculty.name: Maha Farhat
faculty.title: Gilbert S. Omenn Associate Professor of Biomedical Informatics, Harvard Medical School
faculty.summary: Leader in translational genomics and informatics for drug-resistant tuberculosis and other infectious diseases. Work on bacterial genome-wide association studies, phenotype prediction from genomic data, and clinical applications of microbial genomics.

faculty.name: Michael Baym
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Biomedical Informatics, Harvard Medical School
faculty.summary: Leading researcher in microbial evolutionary genomics. Work on visualizing and understanding the evolution of antibiotic resistance. Combines experimental microbiology with computational approaches to study genetic arms races between bacteria and drugs.

faculty.name: Paul Avillach
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Biomedical Informatics, Harvard Medical School
faculty.summary: Researcher in translational bioinformatics focused on integrating multiple heterogeneous data sources (clinical, genomic, environmental) for clinical applications. Work on federated data query systems and clinical decision support.

faculty.name: Pranav Rajpurkar
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Biomedical Informatics, Harvard Medical School
faculty.summary: Leading researcher building reliable AI technologies for biomedical decision-making. Work on medical AI benchmarks, foundation models for medical imaging, and AI systems that match or exceed physician performance on diagnostic tasks. High-impact across medicine and AI.

faculty.name: Marinka Zitnik
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Biomedical Informatics, Harvard Medical School
faculty.summary: Rising star in AI/ML for biomedical networked systems. Work on infusing structure and domain knowledge into machine learning for drug discovery, therapeutic recommendation, and scientific discovery. Pioneer in biomedical knowledge graph methods.

faculty.name: Arjun K. Manrai
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Biomedical Informatics, Harvard Medical School
faculty.summary: Researcher in machine learning for medical decision-making. Work on quantitative approaches to support physicians, addressing bias and health disparities in clinical algorithms, and translating biomedical data science into clinical practice.

faculty.name: Kun-Hsing Yu
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Biomedical Informatics, Harvard Medical School
faculty.summary: Leading researcher in cancer multi-omics integration spanning genomics, epigenomics, transcriptomics, and proteomics. Work on computational pathology and AI-driven cancer diagnostics. Bridges machine learning, molecular biology, and clinical oncology.

faculty.name: Luke O'Connor
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Biomedical Informatics, Harvard Medical School
faculty.summary: Researcher in statistical genetics and complex trait analysis. Work on understanding how genetic variation influences gene regulation and complex disease risk. Developing computational methods for integrative genetic analysis.

funding.note: Fully funded (SEAS PhD guarantee: tuition + stipend)

faculty.name: Leslie G. Valiant
faculty.title: T. Jefferson Coolidge Professor of Computer Science and Applied Mathematics
faculty.summary: Turing Award winner (2010). Pioneered PAC learning model, computational complexity theory, and neuroidal model of the brain. Foundational contributions to theoretical computer science and machine learning theory.

faculty.name: Madhu Sudan
faculty.title: Gordon McKay Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Nevanlinna Prize and ACM Doctoral Dissertation Award winner. Foundational contributions to probabilistically checkable proofs (PCP theorem), list decoding, and property testing. Broad impact across theoretical CS.

faculty.name: Cynthia Dwork
faculty.title: Gordon McKay Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Invented differential privacy, a rigorous framework for privacy-preserving data analysis. Also contributed to algorithmic fairness, distributed consensus, and the Dwork-Levin theorem in complexity theory. Member of NAS, NAE, and AAAS.

faculty.name: Boaz Barak
faculty.title: Catalyst Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Leading researcher at the intersection of cryptography, computational complexity, and machine learning theory. Co-author of the widely used textbook 'Computational Complexity: A Modern Approach.' Work on deep learning theory and adversarial robustness.

faculty.name: Salil P. Vadhan
faculty.title: Vicky Joseph Professor of Computer Science and Applied Mathematics
faculty.summary: Leading researcher in theoretical computer science, cryptography, and data privacy. Director of the Harvard Privacy Tools Project. ACM Fellow. Contributed to complexity theory, zero-knowledge proofs, and privacy-preserving data analysis.

faculty.name: David C. Parkes
faculty.title: John A. Paulson Dean and George F. Colony Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Dean of Harvard SEAS. ACM Fellow and AAAI Fellow. Pioneered research at the intersection of AI, economics, and multi-agent systems. Founded the EconCS group. Recent work on AI Economist and AI-mediated deliberation (Science 2024).

faculty.name: Milind Tambe
faculty.title: Gordon McKay Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: AAAI Fellow and ACM Fellow. Pioneer in AI for social good: applying multi-agent systems and game theory to security, public health, conservation, and social welfare. Director of the Center for Research on Computation and Society.

faculty.name: Sham Kakade
faculty.title: Rampell Family Professor of Computer Science and Professor of Statistics
faculty.summary: Leading machine learning theorist. Contributions to reinforcement learning theory, online learning, tensor methods, and representation learning. Co-director of the Harvard ML Theory group. Highly cited across ML and statistics.

faculty.name: Finale Doshi-Velez
faculty.title: Herchel Smith Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Leading researcher in interpretable and trustworthy machine learning, particularly for healthcare applications. Combines probabilistic modeling with clinical decision support. Pioneer in bridging ML interpretability research with real-world medical deployment.

faculty.name: Ariel Procaccia
faculty.title: Alfred and Rebecca Lin Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Leading researcher in computational social choice and algorithmic economics. Work on fair division, computational voting theory, and AI for democratic decision-making. Known for accessible writing bridging CS and society.

faculty.name: Yiling Chen
faculty.title: Gordon McKay Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Expert in computational economics, social computing, and algorithmic game theory. Research on prediction markets, crowdsourcing mechanisms, and human-AI interaction in economic settings. Bridges economics, AI, and HCI.

faculty.name: Susan A. Murphy
faculty.title: Mallinckrodt Professor of Statistics and of Computer Science
faculty.summary: MacArthur Fellow (2013). Pioneer in sequential decision-making and reinforcement learning for mobile health interventions. Developed the micro-randomized trial design. Bridges statistics, ML, and behavioral health.

faculty.name: Michael D. Mitzenmacher
faculty.title: Thomas J. Watson, Sr. Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: ACM Fellow. Contributions to randomized algorithms, network coding, hash-based data structures (Bloom filters, Count-Min sketch), and coding theory. Co-author of widely used textbook 'Probability and Computing.'

faculty.name: Hanspeter Pfister
faculty.title: An Wang Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Leading researcher in computer graphics, scientific visualization, and computational fabrication. Director of the Visual Computing Group. Work spans volume rendering, bioimage analysis, and deep learning for vision and graphics.

faculty.name: Fernanda Viegas
faculty.title: Gordon McKay Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Pioneer in data visualization and human-centered AI. Co-leads Google's PAIR (People + AI Research) initiative with Martin Wattenberg. Research on interactive visualization for machine learning interpretability and model understanding.

faculty.name: Martin Wattenberg
faculty.title: Gordon McKay Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Pioneer in data visualization and human-centered AI. Co-leads Google's PAIR initiative. Invented treemaps, collaborated on Many Eyes and TensorFlow. Research at the intersection of visualization, machine learning, and user experience.

faculty.name: James Mickens
faculty.title: Gordon McKay Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Leading systems and security researcher. Work on web security, distributed systems, and practical systems design. Known for both deep technical contributions and engaging writing on computer science. Director of the Systems Research Group.

faculty.name: Stratos Idreos
faculty.title: Gordon McKay Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Leading database researcher. Pioneer in self-designing and adaptive data systems. Work on learned indexes, data structures, and storage systems that can automatically adapt to workloads. Director of the Data Systems Lab at Harvard.

faculty.name: Minlan Yu
faculty.title: Gordon McKay Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Leading researcher in networked systems and cloud infrastructure. Work on software-defined networking, programmable data planes, and cloud network management. Award-winning work on efficient network virtualization.

faculty.name: Eddie Kohler
faculty.title: Microsoft Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Operating systems and systems researcher. Co-creator of the Click modular router. Director of Undergraduate Studies in CS. Work on OS design, transactional memory, and reliable distributed systems.

faculty.name: H. T. Kung
faculty.title: William H. Gates Professor of Computer Science and Electrical Engineering
faculty.summary: Pioneer in computer architecture and parallel computing. Known for Kung's method in VLSI systolic arrays, the Kung-Leiserson paper on parallel algorithms, and foundational contributions to computer networks and wireless systems.

faculty.name: David Brooks
faculty.title: Haley Family Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Leading researcher in computer architecture with focus on power-efficient and resilient processor design. Work on voltage scaling, thermal management, and hardware-software co-design for efficient computing systems.

faculty.name: Krzysztof Z. Gajos
faculty.title: Yahn W. Bernier and N. Elizabeth McCaw Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Leading HCI researcher. Work on intelligent interactive systems, accessibility, and personalized user interfaces. Research on adapting interfaces to individual user abilities and designing AI systems that account for human cognitive biases.

faculty.name: Stephen Chong
faculty.title: Gordon McKay Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Leading researcher in programming languages and security. Work on language-based security, information flow control, and formal verification for building secure software systems. Director of Undergraduate Studies in CS.

faculty.name: Nada Amin
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Leading researcher in programming languages, type systems, and compilers. Work on mechanized metatheory, gradual typing, and programming language design. Innovator in making formal methods practical for software development.

faculty.name: Alex Szalay
faculty.title: Bloomberg Distinguished Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Pioneer in data-intensive computing and scientific databases. Architect of the Sloan Digital Sky Survey data system. Leading work on petabyte-scale science databases, spatial data analysis, and applying AI to astrophysics and other sciences. ACM Fellow.

faculty.name: Steven Salzberg
faculty.title: Bloomberg Distinguished Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Leading computational biologist. Pioneered gene-finding algorithms (GLIMMER), genome assembly methods, and metagenomic analysis tools. Work spans human genome analysis, pathogen genomics, and assembly algorithms. Member of NAI. Highly cited across genomics.

faculty.name: Mark Dredze
faculty.title: John C. Malone Professor of Computer Science; Director of the Data Science and AI Institute
faculty.summary: Leading NLP researcher. Director of JHU's Data Science and AI Institute overseeing 150+ affiliated faculty. Work on information extraction, sentiment analysis, and applying NLP to public health surveillance including pioneering work on social media-based disease tracking.

faculty.name: Suchir Saria
faculty.title: John C. Malone Associate Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Leading researcher in machine learning for healthcare. Work on probabilistic graphical models, time series analysis, and predictive modeling for clinical decision support. Founder and CEO of Bayesian Health. High-impact translational ML research.

faculty.name: Alan Yuille
faculty.title: Bloomberg Distinguished Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Pioneering computer vision researcher. Foundational contributions to variational methods, shape analysis, and computational models of vision. Recent work on deep learning for medical image analysis and robust visual recognition. IEEE Fellow.

faculty.name: Raman Arora
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Leading machine learning researcher. Work on statistical learning theory, stochastic optimization, and representation learning with applications to speech and language processing. Focus on theoretical foundations of modern ML methods.

faculty.name: Jason Eisner
faculty.title: Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Leading NLP researcher known for foundational contributions to probabilistic models of language, structured prediction, and the Dyna programming language for declarative NLP. Developed widely-used NLP algorithms and teaching materials.

faculty.name: Philipp Koehn
faculty.title: Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Pioneer in statistical machine translation and author of the standard textbook on the topic. Developed Moses and other widely-used MT systems. Leading research on neural machine translation and multilingual NLP.

faculty.name: David Yarowsky
faculty.title: Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Leading NLP researcher. Pioneered word sense disambiguation methods and cross-lingual NLP. Work on very large text databases, machine translation, and spoken language systems. Long-standing influence on statistical NLP methods.

faculty.name: Benjamin Van Durme
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Leading researcher in NLP and knowledge extraction. Work on scalable semantic processing, event extraction, and building large-scale knowledge bases from text. Contributes to both core NLP and its application to intelligence analysis.

faculty.name: Michael Schatz
faculty.title: Bloomberg Distinguished Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Leading computational biologist and genomics researcher. Work on genome assembly, structural variant detection, and scalable computing for genomics. Contributed to key human genome projects and developed widely-used bioinformatics methods.

faculty.name: Ben Langmead
faculty.title: Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Developer of the Bowtie sequence aligner, one of the most cited bioinformatics tools. Work on efficient algorithms for genomics, text indexing, and high-performance computing applied to biological sequence analysis. Highly cited in genomics and CS.

faculty.name: Alexis Battle
faculty.title: Wu and Zhang Professor of Computer Science; Data Science and AI Institute Director of Research for Strategy and Partnerships
faculty.summary: Leading researcher in genomics and machine learning. Work on statistical models for genetic variation and gene regulation, integrating large-scale genomic data to understand human disease. Builds computational methods at the intersection of ML and genetics.

faculty.name: Gregory D. Hager
faculty.title: Mandell Bellmore Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Leading researcher in computer vision and medical robotics. Work on computer-integrated surgery, human-machine collaborative systems, and robotic perception. Former chair of CS at JHU. Founding director of the Malone Center for Engineering in Healthcare.

faculty.name: Russell Taylor
faculty.title: John C. Malone Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Pioneer in medical robotics and computer-integrated interventional medicine. Developed foundational surgical robot systems. Work on digital twins, human-machine cooperative systems, and medical image-guided interventions. IEEE Fellow and member of NAE.

faculty.name: Misha Kazhdan
faculty.title: Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Leading researcher in computer graphics and geometry processing. Known for Poisson surface reconstruction and foundational work on 3D shape analysis, discrete differential geometry, and efficient algorithms for graphics.

faculty.name: Randal Burns
faculty.title: Bill and Lisa Stromberg Department Head and Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Department Head of CS. Leading researcher in large-scale data systems for science. Work on scalable neuroscience imaging infrastructure, redundancy coding for storage, adaptive performance management, and scientific data management at petabyte scale.

faculty.name: Vladimir Braverman
faculty.title: Professor of Computer Science; Associate Head of Faculty
faculty.summary: Leading researcher in algorithms for massive datasets. Work on streaming algorithms, sketching techniques, and their applications to machine learning and healthcare. Contributes to both theoretical foundations and practical deployment of scalable ML.

faculty.name: Michael Dinitz
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Leading theoretical computer scientist. Work on approximation algorithms, network design, and distributed computing. Known for contributions to fault-tolerant network design and metric embeddings. Bridges theory with practical algorithmic engineering.

faculty.name: Xin Li
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Leading researcher in theoretical computer science. Work on computational complexity, pseudorandomness, coding theory, and their intersection with cryptography. Contributes to both fundamental theory and practical cryptographic constructions.

faculty.name: Matthew Green
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Leading cryptography and security researcher. Work on applied cryptography, privacy-enhancing technologies, and cryptocurrency security. Known for contributions to zero-knowledge proofs, anonymous credentials, and secure systems design.

faculty.name: Abhishek Jain
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Leading cryptographer. Work on foundational cryptographic primitives, secure multi-party computation, and blockchain security. Contributions to both theoretical cryptography and practical secure systems.

faculty.name: Yinzhi Cao
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Computer Science; Technical Director of JHU Information Security Institute
faculty.summary: Leading security researcher and Technical Director of the JHU Information Security Institute. Work on security and privacy of machine learning, web and mobile system security, and payment system security. Bridges systems and ML security.

faculty.name: Scott Smith
faculty.title: Professor of Computer Science; Director of Graduate Studies
faculty.summary: Leading programming languages researcher. Work on type systems, formal verification, and programming language design. Director of Graduate Studies in CS. Focus on making formal methods practical for software development.

faculty.name: Ilya Shpitser
faculty.title: John C. Malone Associate Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Leading researcher in causal inference and missing data. Work on graphical models, semi-parametric inference, and algorithmic fairness. Applies causal methods to healthcare, public health, and social science problems.

funding.note: Fully funded (all Rossin College PhDs 100% funded); P3 Fellowship available

faculty.name: Brian D. Davison
faculty.title: Professor and Chair; Daniel E. '39 and Patricia M. Smith Endowed Chair, Computer Science and Engineering
faculty.summary: Department Chair and leading researcher in data science, web search, and information retrieval. Work on machine learning and data mining with applications in text, the Web, and social networks. Co-founder of the MS in Data Science program. NSF CAREER awardee.

faculty.name: Daniel P. Lopresti
faculty.title: Professor of Computer Science and Engineering; Interim Dean, Rossin College of Engineering
faculty.summary: Distinguished researcher in pattern recognition and document analysis systems. Work on electronic voting security, cybersecurity, and bioinformatics. Interim Dean of the Rossin College of Engineering and Applied Science. ACM Distinguished Scientist.

faculty.name: Mooi Choo Chuah
faculty.title: Professor of Computer Science and Engineering
faculty.summary: IEEE Fellow and NAI Fellow. Leading researcher in network and cyber-physical system security, mobile healthcare, and assistive technologies. Holds 62+ US patents. Previously Distinguished Member of Technical Staff at Bell Labs. Associate Director of I-DISC.

faculty.name: Jeff Heflin
faculty.title: Professor of Computer Science and Engineering; Director, Cognitive Science Program
faculty.summary: Leading researcher in artificial intelligence, the Semantic Web, and knowledge representation. Work on ontologies, intelligent agents, and web-based knowledge systems. Director of the Cognitive Science Program.

faculty.name: Henry Korth
faculty.title: Professor of Computer Science and Engineering
faculty.summary: Leading researcher in database systems, blockchain technology, and distributed systems. Work on zero-knowledge proofs, consensus algorithms, and blockchain benchmarking. Co-director of the Computer Science and Business (CSB) program. Co-author of the widely-used 'Database System Concepts' textbook.

faculty.name: Michael Spear
faculty.title: Professor of Computer Science and Engineering; Director of Master's Program
faculty.summary: Leading researcher in concurrency, parallel computing, and programming language design. Work on transactional memory, multicore synchronization, and cloud computing infrastructure. Director of the MS program in CSE.

faculty.name: Parv Venkitasubramaniam
faculty.title: Professor of Computer Science and Engineering
faculty.summary: Leading researcher in privacy and security, with focus on the fundamental tradeoffs between privacy and performance in networked systems. Work on information-theoretic privacy, cyber-physical security, and statistical data privacy.

faculty.name: Roberto Palmieri
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Computer Science and Engineering
faculty.summary: Leading researcher in distributed computing and systems. Work on concurrent data structures, transactional memory, and fault-tolerant distributed protocols. Focus on cluster and geographic replication for high-performance and reliable systems.

faculty.name: Eric P. S. Baumer
faculty.title: Professor of Computer Science and Engineering
faculty.summary: Leading researcher in human-computer interaction and social informatics. NSF CAREER awardee. Work on participatory methods for human-centered design of algorithmic systems, natural language processing, and critical studies of algorithms in society.

faculty.name: Hector Munoz-Avila
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Computer Science and Engineering
faculty.summary: NSF CAREER awardee. Researcher in agentic AI, hierarchical task network (HTN) planning, and case-based reasoning. Current work on AI grounding, AI alignment, and combining hierarchical representations with large language models. Lehigh Class of 1961 Professor.

faculty.name: Lifang He
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Computer Science and Engineering
faculty.summary: Rising researcher in machine learning and data science. Work on tensor analysis methods and their applications to biomedical informatics and neuroimaging. IEEE Senior Member. Tenured in 2025. Multiple NSF, NIH, and DOE grants.

faculty.name: Brian Y. Chen
faculty.title: Professor of Computer Science and Engineering; PhD Program Director
faculty.summary: Researcher in structural bioinformatics and computational biology. Work on algorithms for protein structure analysis and biomolecular recognition. Director of the PhD program in CSE. Bridges computer science, biology, and chemistry.

faculty.name: Aparna Bharati
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Computer Science and Engineering
faculty.summary: Rising researcher in computer vision and multimedia forensics. Work on deep learning methods for image manipulation detection, biometrics, and media integrity verification. Leads the Lehigh Computer Vision Group (LCVG). PhD from Notre Dame (2020).

faculty.name: David Saldaña
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Computer Science and Engineering
faculty.summary: Rising researcher in AI and robotics. Work on autonomous aerial robots, resilient robot swarms, and environmental monitoring applications. Focus on making robot teams robust to failures in challenging environments.

faculty.name: Sihong Xie
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Computer Science and Engineering
faculty.summary: Researcher in data mining and machine learning. Work on crowdsourcing, text and opinion mining, and mining noisy and biased data. Focus on making data-driven systems more reliable and trustworthy.

funding.note: Fully funded (full tuition, stipend, medical insurance)

faculty.name: Leslie Kaelbling
faculty.title: Panasonic Professor of Computer Science and Engineering
faculty.summary: Leader in AI, reinforcement learning, and robot learning. Panasonic Professor and MIT AI+D faculty member. Research spans planning under uncertainty, sensorimotor learning, and foundational AI.

faculty.name: Daniela Rus
faculty.title: Director of CSAIL; Andrew (1956) and Erna Viterbi Professor
faculty.summary: Director of MIT CSAIL. Research in robotics, AI, and autonomous systems. Leads work on soft robotics, self-driving vehicles, and distributed robotic networks.

faculty.name: Antonio Torralba
faculty.title: Faculty Head of AI+D; Delta Electronics Professor
faculty.summary: Faculty Head of MIT AI+D initiative. World-renowned for computer vision, scene understanding, and deep learning research. Work spans visual recognition, human perception modeling, and multimodal AI.

faculty.name: Regina Barzilay
faculty.title: School of Engineering Distinguished Professor for AI and Health
faculty.summary: Distinguished Professor pioneering AI for healthcare. Research in natural language processing, ML for drug discovery, and clinical AI. MacArthur Fellow and AAAI Fellow.

faculty.name: Tommi Jaakkola
faculty.title: Thomas M. Siebel Distinguished Professor
faculty.summary: Distinguished Professor in statistical machine learning, inference, and ML for molecular design. Work spans generative models, adversarial robustness, and computational biology.

faculty.name: Aleksander Mądry
faculty.title: Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Leader in trustworthy AI and ML theory. Faculty lead of the Center for Deployable Machine Learning. Research in robustness, fairness, and foundations of modern ML paradigms.

faculty.name: Kaiming He
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Pioneer in deep learning and computer vision. Creator of ResNet (over 200K citations), one of the most influential architectures in deep learning history. Research in self-supervised learning and representation learning.

faculty.name: Constantinos Daskalakis
faculty.title: Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Leading theoretical computer scientist. Nevanlinna Prize winner (2018). Research in algorithmic game theory, equilibrium computation, and foundations of machine learning with applications to statistics.

faculty.name: Erik Demaine
faculty.title: Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Renowned algorithms researcher, MacArthur Fellow. Research in computational origami, data structures, graph algorithms, and combinatorial geometry. Widely published across theory and recreational mathematics.

faculty.name: Piotr Indyk
faculty.title: Thomas D. and Virginia W. Cabot Professor
faculty.summary: Leading expert in streaming algorithms, high-dimensional computational geometry, and sublinear algorithms. Co-inventor of locality-sensitive hashing. Extensive impact on big data algorithmics.

faculty.name: Ankur Moitra
faculty.title: Norbert Wiener Professor of Mathematics and Computer Science
faculty.summary: Norbert Wiener Professor working at the intersection of algorithms, ML, and statistics. Research in tensor methods, nonnegative matrix factorization, and theoretical foundations of deep learning.

faculty.name: Samuel Madden
faculty.title: Distinguished Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Distinguished Professor and leader in database systems, big data infrastructure, and ML systems. Research in data management for analytics, cloud computing, and IoT data platforms.

faculty.name: Hari Balakrishnan
faculty.title: Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Pioneer in networked systems, wireless networking, and mobile computing. Co-founder of Cambridge Mobile Telematics. Research in networking protocols, distributed systems, and IoT.

faculty.name: Dina Katabi
faculty.title: Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: MacArthur Fellow and leader in wireless networks and AI for healthcare. Research in wireless sensing, ML for medical diagnostics, and networking. Inventor of Emerald device for contactless health monitoring.

faculty.name: M. Frans Kaashoek
faculty.title: Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Leading operating systems and distributed systems researcher. Co-creator of the exokernel concept and influential contributions to OS design, distributed storage, and secure software systems. ACM Fellow.

faculty.name: Barbara Liskov
faculty.title: Institute Professor
faculty.summary: Institute Professor and Turing Award winner (2008). Inventor of the Liskov substitution principle, a foundational concept in object-oriented programming. Pioneered abstract data types and distributed computing.

faculty.name: Tim Kraska
faculty.title: Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Leader in learned database systems and ML for data management. Research in instance-optimized data systems, generative AI for databases, and learned index structures.

faculty.name: Srini Devadas
faculty.title: Edwin Sibley Webster Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Leader in computer security, hardware security, and cryptography. Research in secure processors, trusted computing, and cryptographic protocols. ACM Fellow.

faculty.name: Vinod Vaikuntanathan
faculty.title: Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Leading cryptographer working on lattice-based cryptography, fully homomorphic encryption, and quantum-secure cryptographic constructions. Research also in ML privacy.

faculty.name: Yael Kalai
faculty.title: Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Leading researcher in cryptography and computational complexity. Research focuses on succinct proofs, delegation of computation, and foundational cryptographic constructions. Invited speaker at ICM.

faculty.name: Pulkit Agrawal
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Leading researcher in robot learning and sensorimotor control. Research in reinforcement learning for robotics, curiosity-driven learning, and sim-to-real transfer.

faculty.name: Jacob Andreas
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Rising star in NLP and language grounding (newly tenured 2026). Research in language-guided policy learning, interpretable NLP models, and compositional generalization.

faculty.name: Stefanie Jegelka
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Leading researcher in ML and optimization with focus on graph neural networks, kernel methods, and discrete-continuous optimization. Regular contributions to NeurIPS, ICML, and COLT.

faculty.name: David Sontag
faculty.title: Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Leader in probabilistic graphical models, causal inference, and AI for healthcare. Research applies ML to clinical decision support, disease progression modeling, and health policy.

faculty.name: Mohammad Alizadeh
faculty.title: Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Leader in networked systems with contributions spanning programmable networks, datacenter networking, and ML for systems. Research bridges systems, networking, and ML.

funding.note: Fully funded via MacCracken (full tuition + stipend + health insurance, 4-5 yrs + 3 summers)

faculty.name: Yann LeCun
faculty.title: Jacob T. Schwartz Chaired Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Turing Award winner (2018) and Chief AI Scientist at Meta. Pioneer of convolutional neural networks and deep learning. Research in self-supervised learning, energy-based models, and autonomous machine intelligence.

faculty.name: Joan Bruna
faculty.title: Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Leading researcher in deep learning theory, geometric deep learning, and high-dimensional statistics. Work spans graph neural networks, unsupervised learning, and ML for scientific computing.

faculty.name: Kyunghyun Cho
faculty.title: Glen de Vries Chaired Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Leading NLP researcher. Inventor of the attention mechanism (NeurIPS 2014) foundational to modern LLMs. Research in neural machine translation, language modeling, and text generation.

faculty.name: Andrew Gordon Wilson
faculty.title: Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Leading researcher in Bayesian deep learning, probabilistic modeling, and deep learning theory. Creator of GPyTorch. Research in uncertainty quantification, out-of-distribution generalization, and LLMs.

faculty.name: Julia Kempe
faculty.title: Silver Professor of Computer Science and Data Science
faculty.summary: Silver Professor bridging ML, quantum computing, and data science. Research in deep learning theory, AI alignment, representation learning, and quantum information theory.

faculty.name: Mehryar Mohri
faculty.title: Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Leading ML theorist and author of the authoritative textbook 'Foundations of Machine Learning.' Research in learning theory, online learning, algorithmic fairness, and computational biology.

faculty.name: Sanjeev Khanna
faculty.title: Silver Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Silver Professor and leader in approximation algorithms, combinatorial optimization, and sublinear algorithms. ACM Fellow and Guggenheim Fellow. Extensive work on network design and matching problems.

faculty.name: Subhash Khot
faculty.title: Silver Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Silver Professor and Nevanlinna Prize winner (2014). Creator of the Unique Games Conjecture, which revolutionized the study of hardness of approximation in theoretical computer science.

faculty.name: Oded Regev
faculty.title: Silver Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Silver Professor and leader in lattice-based cryptography and quantum computation. Inventor of Learning With Errors (LWE), the foundation of post-quantum cryptography. Godel Prize winner.

faculty.name: Yevgeniy Dodis
faculty.title: Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Leading cryptographer with contributions to leakage-resilient cryptography, key derivation, and information-theoretic security. Extensive publications at CRYPTO, EUROCRYPT, and TCC.

faculty.name: Martin Farach-Colton
faculty.title: Leonard J. Shustek Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Leading researcher in data structures and algorithms for storage systems. Contributions to B-tree variants, graph algorithms, and streaming computation. ACM Fellow.

faculty.name: Anupam Gupta
faculty.title: Silver Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Silver Professor with contributions to approximation algorithms, metric embeddings, and online algorithms. Research bridges theoretical CS foundations with practical algorithmic design.

faculty.name: Rob Fergus
faculty.title: Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Leading computer vision researcher. Co-founder of the influential research direction in unsupervised visual representation learning. Research spans deep generative models, visual understanding, and self-supervised learning.

faculty.name: Jinyang Li
faculty.title: Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Leader in ML systems and distributed computing. Research in efficient large model training, LLM serving systems, and out-of-core graph neural network training. Recent publications at OSDI, NSDI, SIGMOD, Eurosys.

faculty.name: Justin Cappos
faculty.title: Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Leader in software supply chain security and systems. Creator of The Update Framework (TUF), a CNCF graduated project securing software updates at major tech companies. Research in secure systems architecture.

faculty.name: Juliana Freire
faculty.title: Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Leader in data management, visualization, and reproducibility. Director of the NYU Center for Data Science. Research in urban analytics, scientific data analysis, and provenance management.

faculty.name: Thomas Wies
faculty.title: Professor of Computer Science; Department Chair
faculty.summary: Department Chair and leader in program analysis and formal verification. Research in automated reasoning, concurrent program verification, and making formal methods practical for software engineering.

faculty.name: Greg Durrett
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Rising NLP researcher who joined NYU in 2025. Research in LLMs, textual reasoning, and information extraction. Sloan Fellow with extensive publications at ACL, EMNLP, NeurIPS.

faculty.name: He He
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Rising researcher in NLP and ML focusing on text generation, robust language understanding, and human-AI collaboration. Research in controllable generation and adversarial robustness.

faculty.name: Samuel Bowman
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Computer Science (on leave 2025-2026)
faculty.summary: Leader in AI evaluation and safety. Creator of widely-used NLP benchmarks (GLUE, SuperGLUE). Research in understanding and evaluating LLM capabilities, robustness, and alignment. Currently at Anthropic.

faculty.name: Rajesh Ranganath
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Leader in ML for healthcare with contributions to deep probabilistic models and clinical prediction. Research in causal inference, variational methods, and deploying ML in clinical settings.

faculty.name: Lerrel Pinto
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Rising star in robot learning. Research in reinforcement learning for dexterous manipulation, sim-to-real transfer, and sample-efficient robot learning. Packard Fellow.

faculty.name: Matus Telgarsky
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Leading deep learning theorist. Research in understanding gradient-based optimization of neural networks, implicit bias, and generalization properties of overparameterized models.

faculty.name: Saining Xie
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Rising computer vision researcher. Research in self-supervised visual learning, scalable vision architectures, and multi-modal learning. Creator of influential vision models and benchmarks.

faculty.name: Dennis Shasha
faculty.title: Silver Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Silver Professor with broad contributions across databases, algorithms, and computational biology. Research in network inference, time series analysis, and privacy-preserving computation.

faculty.name: Amal Ahmed
faculty.title: Professor, Associate Dean of Graduate Programs
faculty.summary: Professor and Associate Dean. Research in programming languages, semantics, type systems, secure compilation, gradual typing, and software contracts.

faculty.name: Javed Aslam
faculty.title: Professor, Chief of Artificial Intelligence
faculty.summary: Professor and Chief of AI. Research in machine learning and information retrieval, with work in human computation, transportation, computer security, wireless networking, and medical informatics.

faculty.name: David Bau
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Assistant Professor. Research centers on human-computer interaction and machine learning, including the gap between AI efficacy and scientists' ability to explain it. Lead PI of the National Deep Inference Fabric (NDIF).

faculty.name: Soheil Behnezhad
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Assistant Professor broadly interested in theoretical computer science, especially graph algorithms and the theoretical foundations of big data algorithms.

faculty.name: Jonathan Bell
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Associate Professor. Research focuses on automated approaches to help developers write better software, particularly in Java and JavaScript ecosystems.

faculty.name: Enrico Bertini
faculty.title: Associate Professor, Jointly with CAMD
faculty.summary: Associate Professor working to make data visualization more understandable and useful for broad audiences. Research at the intersection of visualization and machine learning.

faculty.name: Carla Brodley
faculty.title: Professor, Former Dean
faculty.summary: Professor and former Dean (2014-2021) who hired 46 tenure/tenure-track faculty. Research in computing education, broadening participation in CS, and machine learning.

faculty.name: Seth Cooper
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Associate Professor and pioneer of scientific discovery games. Combines game design with crowdsourcing for scientific problem-solving.

faculty.name: Gene Cooperman
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Professor leading the High Performance Computing Laboratory. Research in interdisciplinary computing, math, and physics topics including fault tolerance and formal verification.

faculty.name: Peter Desnoyers
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Associate Professor researching storage issues in operating systems and integration of emerging storage tech into existing software infrastructures. Co-founder of Massachusetts Open Cloud.

faculty.name: Jennifer Dy
faculty.title: Professor, Jointly with College of Engineering
faculty.summary: Professor. Research in machine learning, data mining, statistical pattern recognition, and computer vision.

faculty.name: Ehsan Elhamifar
faculty.title: Associate Professor, Jointly with College of Engineering
faculty.summary: Associate Professor developing AI that learns from and makes inferences about visual data. Research in computer vision, machine learning, and AI.

faculty.name: Yun Raymond Fu
faculty.title: Professor, University Distinguished Professor, Jointly with Engineering
faculty.summary: Professor and University Distinguished Professor. Research in AI, machine learning, computer vision, and data mining. Over 500 scientific publications and 40+ patented inventions.

faculty.name: Wolfgang Gatterbauer
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Associate Professor working on the theory of scalable data management, aiming to expand data management systems with novel functionalities.

faculty.name: Fatemeh Ghoreishi
faculty.title: Assistant Professor, Jointly with College of Engineering
faculty.summary: Assistant Professor examining machine learning and Bayesian statistics for design and decision-making under uncertainty.

faculty.name: Arjun Guha
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Associate Professor. Research in programming languages, type systems, and software engineering.

faculty.name: Huaizu Jiang
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Assistant Professor. Research in computer vision, computational photography, and machine learning.

faculty.name: Karl Lieberherr
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Professor. Research in modularity in software, test-driven development, and adaptive programming.

faculty.name: Stacy Marsella
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Professor. Research in computational modeling of cognition, emotion, and social behavior, with applications in virtual agents and human-AI interaction.

faculty.name: Huy Lê Nguyen
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Associate Professor. Research in algorithmic techniques for massive data, optimization, and machine learning.

faculty.name: Jose Perea
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Associate Professor. Research in data science, machine learning, topological data analysis, and algorithms.

faculty.name: Mirek Riedewald
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Professor and co-founder of the DATA Lab at Northeastern. Research in data management systems and big data analytics.

faculty.name: Ji-Yong Shin
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Assistant Professor. Research in distributed systems, formal verification, and cloud storage reliability.

faculty.name: Frank Tip
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Professor. Research in programming languages, software engineering, program analysis, and automated refactoring.

faculty.name: Emanuele Viola
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Professor. Research in theoretical computer science, computational complexity, lower bounds, and pseudorandomness.

faculty.name: Jan Vitek
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Professor. Research in programming languages, compilers, type systems, and runtime systems.

faculty.name: Abhi Shelat
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Professor in cryptography. Ph.D. from MIT. Previously tenured at University of Virginia. NSF CAREER award, Microsoft Faculty Fellowship. Research in applied cryptography and secure computation.

faculty.name: Daniel Wichs
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Professor in cryptography. Research on computing on encrypted data, program obfuscation, lattice-based cryptography, and information-theoretic cryptography. Senior Scientist at NTT Research.

faculty.name: Alina Oprea
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Professor. Research at the intersection of AI and cybersecurity, including adversarial ML, trustworthy AI, privacy in ML, security for LLM agents, and cloud security. Google Security and Privacy Award (2019).

faculty.name: Cristina Nita-Rotaru
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Professor and founding member of the Cybersecurity and Privacy Institute. Research in security of network protocols and distributed systems, adversarial testing, formal verification, and blockchains. NSF CAREER (2006).

faculty.name: Engin Kirda
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Professor. Research in systems, software, and network security, malware detection and analysis, and web application security.

faculty.name: David Choffnes
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Professor. Research to improve the privacy, security, performance, and reliability of internet systems. Former Executive Director of the Cybersecurity and Privacy Institute. NSF CAREER awardee.

faculty.name: Alan Mislove
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Professor. Research in network measurement and privacy/security in online social networks.

faculty.name: Christo Wilson
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Professor. Research in algorithm auditing, online tracking, privacy, and platform accountability.

faculty.name: Guevara Noubir
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Professor. Research in privacy and security, robustness of networked systems, and wireless security.

faculty.name: Jonathan Ullman
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Associate Professor researching foundations of privacy for ML and statistics, differential privacy, and cryptography. Member of Theory Group and Cybersecurity and Privacy Institute. NSF CAREER awardee.

faculty.name: Long Lu
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Associate Professor and co-director of SecLab. Research in low-level software security, embedded and IoT/CPS systems security, program analysis, and trusted computing. NSF CAREER awardee.

faculty.name: Ziming Zhao
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Associate Professor. Research in systems and software security, network and web security, and human-centric security. NSF CAREER Award, ACM SACMAT Test-of-Time Award 2024.

faculty.name: Tianshi Li
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Assistant Professor. Directs the PEACH (Privacy-Enabling AI and Computer-Human interaction) Lab. Core faculty at Cybersecurity and Privacy Institute. Research in HCI, privacy, AI, and LLM privacy issues.

faculty.name: Jayshree Sarathy
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Assistant Professor. Member of Cybersecurity and Privacy Institute. Research in privacy, data science, and sociotechnical dynamics of responsible data science.

faculty.name: Laura Edelson
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Assistant Professor in cybersecurity and privacy and human-centered computing. Studies the spread of harmful content through large online networks.

faculty.name: Kevin Fu
faculty.title: Professor, Jointly with College of Engineering
faculty.summary: Professor jointly appointed with Engineering. Research seeks to improve the security of embedded systems and devices, particularly in healthcare. Pioneer in medical device cybersecurity.

faculty.name: Joshua Gancher
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Assistant Professor. Aims to mathematically verify the security of foundational software and create tools to do that process at scale.

faculty.name: Elettra Bietti
faculty.title: Assistant Professor, Jointly with School of Law
faculty.summary: Assistant Professor jointly appointed with Law. Researches how technology overlaps with data law, privacy, and antitrust laws in the digital economy.

faculty.name: Don Fallis
faculty.title: Professor, Jointly with CSSH
faculty.summary: Professor jointly appointed with CSSH. Studies adversarial epistemology -- how people learn in a disinformation-filled world.

faculty.name: Timothy W. Bickmore
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Professor and Director of the Relational Agents Group. Develops computer agents for health provider-patient interactions. Research in HCI, natural language processing, and animation for health counseling. Ph.D. from MIT Media Lab (2003). NSF CAREER awardee. Top 2% most-cited scientists worldwide.

faculty.name: Elizabeth Mynatt
faculty.title: Dean and Professor
faculty.summary: Dean of Khoury College. Research in human-computer interaction, health informatics, and ubiquitous computing with focus on aging-in-place and chronic disease self-management.

faculty.name: Stephen Intille
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.sub_field: Personal Health Technologies and Wearable Sensing
faculty.summary: Professor. Research on personal health technologies, wearable sensing, behavioral informatics, and mobile health interventions. Develops sensor-based systems for continuous health monitoring.

faculty.name: Aarti Sathyanarayana
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Assistant Professor. Research in digital health, wearable devices, digital biomarkers, and machine learning for health monitoring.

faculty.name: Herman Saksono
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Assistant Professor. Research in human-centered computing and health informatics, with focus on health equity and community-based health interventions.

faculty.name: Dakuo Wang
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Associate Professor. Research in HCI, human-AI collaboration, NLP, and health informatics. Focus on designing AI systems that support healthcare work.

faculty.name: Predrag Radivojac
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Professor. Research in bioinformatics, machine learning, and proteomics applied to health informatics and precision medicine. Develops computational methods for biological and clinical data.

faculty.name: Maitraye Das
faculty.title: Assistant Professor, Jointly with CAMD
faculty.summary: Assistant Professor jointly with CAMD. HCI research on equity and accessibility gaps in education, employment, and creative work, including health-related contexts.

faculty.name: Armando Cortez
faculty.title: Part-Time Lecturer, Health Informatics
faculty.summary: Part-Time Lecturer and CIO at Harbor Health Services. Focus on co-evolution of healthcare and technology. Brings industry perspective on health informatics.

faculty.name: Byron Wallace
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Associate Professor. Research in data mining, machine learning, and NLP with applications to health informatics and evidence-based medicine. Focus on automating systematic reviews and clinical text mining.

faculty.name: Albert-László Barabási
faculty.title: Robert Gray Dodge Professor of Network Science, University Distinguished Professor
faculty.summary: Distinguished Professor who discovered scale-free networks and developed the Barabási-Albert preferential attachment model. Director of Center for Complex Network Research. Elected to National Academy of Sciences (2024). Appointments at Harvard Medical School and Dana-Farber Cancer Institute. Author of 'Linked' and 'The Formula'.

faculty.name: Tina Eliassi-Rad
faculty.title: Inaugural Joseph E. Aoun Professor
faculty.summary: Inaugural Joseph E. Aoun Professor. Core faculty of Network Science Institute and Institute for Experiential AI. Research at intersection of data mining, machine learning, and network science.

faculty.name: David Lazer
faculty.title: University Distinguished Professor, Jointly with CSSH
faculty.sub_field: Computational Social Science and Network Science
faculty.summary: University Distinguished Professor and core faculty of the Network Science Institute. Research in computational social science and network science, studying social and political networks at scale.

faculty.name: Cody Dunne
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Associate Professor. Research in information visualization, network science, and human-computer interaction. Strives to make network data and visualizations easier to analyze and share.

faculty.name: Alan Mislove
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Professor. Research in network measurement and online social networks, studying the structure and dynamics of large-scale online platforms.

faculty.name: Christo Wilson
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Professor. Research in algorithm auditing, online platform dynamics, and how algorithmic systems shape networked information flows.

faculty.name: Christoph Riedl
faculty.title: Professor, Jointly with D'Amore-McKim School of Business
faculty.summary: Professor. Research in collective intelligence, human-AI teaming, and crowdsourcing, studying how networked collaboration shapes outcomes.

faculty.name: Alessandro Vespignani
faculty.title: Sternberg Family Distinguished University Professor, Jointly with COS
faculty.summary: Sternberg Family Distinguished University Professor. Research in complex networks, computational epidemiology, and epidemic modeling. Core faculty of the Network Science Institute.

faculty.name: Ricardo Baeza-Yates
faculty.title: Professor of the Practice
faculty.summary: Professor of the Practice. Research in information retrieval, web search, AI, and web science with applications to network analysis. Former VP of Research at Yahoo.

faculty.name: Mahsa Derakhshan
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Assistant Professor. Research in design and analysis of algorithms, specifically algorithms under uncertainty with applications to networked systems.

faculty.name: Samir Khuller
faculty.title: Peter and Adrienne Barris Chair; Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Department Chair and leading algorithms researcher. Contributions to approximation algorithms, graph algorithms, scheduling, and fairness in clustering. Over 9,700 citations. Previously served as Elizabeth Iribe Chair at Maryland.

faculty.name: Kristian Hammond
faculty.title: Bill and Cathy Osborn Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Director of the MS in AI program and Director of the Center for Advancing Safety of Machine Intelligence (CASMI). Research focuses on operationalizing AI safety and human-centered AI systems.

faculty.name: Ken Forbus
faculty.title: Walter P. Murphy Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Leading AI researcher in qualitative reasoning, analogical reasoning, and cognitive architectures. Research focuses on building AI systems that reason from limited data like humans do. Extensive work on sketch understanding and spatial reasoning.

faculty.name: Han Liu
faculty.title: Orrington Lunt Professor of Computer Science and Statistics
faculty.summary: Leader in statistical machine learning. Research in high-dimensional statistics, nonparametric learning, representation learning, and deep learning foundations. Joint appointment in Statistics.

faculty.name: Jason Hartline
faculty.title: Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Leading algorithmic game theory researcher. Contributions to mechanism design, auction theory, regulation of algorithmic collusion, and no-regret learning in games. Over 4,200 citations.

faculty.name: Jessica Hullman
faculty.title: Ginni Rometty Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Ginni Rometty Professor and leader in visualization and HCI. Research in uncertainty representation, human-AI decision making, and formal models of inference with data. Multiple Best Paper awards at IEEE VIS and ACM CHI. NSF CAREER awardee.

faculty.name: Peter Dinda
faculty.title: Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Leader in operating systems and high-performance computing. Research in virtualization, OS design, and runtime systems for parallel and distributed computing. Directs the Prescience Lab.

faculty.name: Fabian Bustamante
faculty.title: Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Leading researcher in internet measurement, distributed systems, and networked systems. Research in CDNs, peer-to-peer systems, and understanding internet infrastructure at global scale. Directs the Aqualab.

faculty.name: Aleksandar Kuzmanovic
faculty.title: Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Leader in networking and internet architecture. Research in network protocols, internet security, cloud networking, and network neutrality. Directs the Northwestern Networks Group.

faculty.name: Nikos Hardavellas
faculty.title: Professor of Computer Science; Associate Chair for Graduate Studies
faculty.summary: Associate Chair for Graduate Studies (PhD). Research in computer architecture, parallel systems, and datacenter-scale computing. Focus on energy-efficient architecture and emerging technology systems.

faculty.name: Edith Elkind
faculty.title: Ginni Rometty Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Ginni Rometty Professor and leader in computational social choice and algorithmic game theory. Research in voting systems, fair division, and multi-agent AI systems. ACM Distinguished Member.

faculty.name: Konstantin Makarychev
faculty.title: Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Leading algorithms researcher. Contributions to metric embeddings, approximation algorithms for clustering and graph partitioning, and the theoretical foundations of data science.

faculty.name: Aravindan Vijayaraghavan
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Leading researcher in theoretical foundations of data science and ML. Research in tensor methods, non-convex optimization, and provable algorithms for unsupervised learning. Affiliated with NSF IDEAL institute.

faculty.name: V.S. Subrahmanian
faculty.title: Walter P. Murphy Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Walter P. Murphy Professor and leader in AI for security applications. Co-Director of the Northwestern Network for Collaborative Intelligence. Research in cyber threat prediction, geopolitical forecasting, and probabilistic logic.

faculty.name: Xinyu Xing
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Leading systems security researcher. Research in automated vulnerability discovery, binary analysis, and AI-driven security testing. Active in finding critical vulnerabilities in widely-used software.

faculty.name: Xiao Wang
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Leading cryptographer focused on zero-knowledge proofs and secure multi-party computation. Research in practical verifiable computation and privacy-preserving protocols.

faculty.name: Robby Findler
faculty.title: Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Leader in programming languages and software engineering. Core contributor to the Racket programming language ecosystem. Research in contract systems, gradual typing, and program verification tools.

faculty.name: Christos Dimoulas
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Researcher in programming languages theory and design. Research in program analysis, behavioral software contracts, and meta-programming. Co-developer of the Racket PLT research ecosystem.

faculty.name: Matthew Kay
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Computer Science and Communication Studies
faculty.summary: Leader in HCI and visualization. Research in uncertainty communication, personal informatics, and designing data-driven technologies. Co-director of the Midwest Uncertainty Collective. Multiple best paper awards at CHI and VIS.

faculty.name: Brenna Argall
faculty.title: Professor of Computer Science, Mechanical Engineering, and Physical Medicine & Rehabilitation
faculty.summary: Leader in assistive robotics and human-robot interaction. Research in autonomous wheelchair control, robot-assisted rehabilitation, and shared autonomy systems for people with motor impairments.

faculty.name: Emma Alexander
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Rising computer vision researcher. Research in computational imaging, bio-inspired vision systems, and ML-driven image reconstruction. Builds cameras and algorithms inspired by biological vision.

faculty.name: Manling Li
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Rising NLP researcher. Research in knowledge-based AI, language understanding and generation, and multimodal reasoning. Focus on building systems that ground language in structured knowledge.

faculty.name: Miklos Racz
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Computer Science and Statistics
faculty.summary: Leading researcher in network science and statistics. Research in graph limits, network inference, ranking, and the intersection of probability theory with data science and machine learning.

faculty.name: Simone Campanoni
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Leader in compiler design and program analysis. Research in automatic parallelization, compiler optimizations for emerging architectures, and tools for program correctness and performance.

faculty.name: Bryan Pardo
faculty.title: Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Leader in machine audition and audio AI. Research in sound source separation, automatic music transcription, and explainable audio ML. Directs the Interactive Audio Lab.

funding.note: Fully funded: full tuition, health plan, and base stipend for all Ph.D. students throughout regular enrollment

faculty.name: Sanjeev Arora
faculty.title: Charles C. Fitzmorris Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Leading theoretical computer scientist. Godel Prize and ACM Prize winner. Pioneered the PCP theorem. Current research in theoretical foundations of deep learning, optimization for ML, and understanding LLMs.

faculty.name: Jennifer Rexford
faculty.title: Gordon Y.S. Wu Professor of Computer Science; Provost
faculty.summary: Provost of Princeton and world-leading networking researcher. Pioneer of software-defined networking and network programmability. ACM Fellow and member of the National Academy of Engineering.

faculty.name: Robert Tarjan
faculty.title: James S. McDonnell Distinguished University Professor
faculty.summary: Turing Award winner (1986). Inventor of foundational graph algorithms (Tarjan's SCC, union-find, Fibonacci heaps) and seminal contributions to algorithm design and data structures. NAE member.

faculty.name: Margaret Martonosi
faculty.title: William M. Addy '82 University Professor; Associate Chair
faculty.summary: University Professor and leader in computer architecture, mobile systems, and hardware-software interfaces. Research spans power-aware computing, quantum architecture, and mobile sensing. NAE member and ACM Fellow.

faculty.name: Arvind Narayanan
faculty.title: Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Leader in privacy, security, and tech policy. Author of acclaimed books on AI ethics. Research in de-anonymization, blockchain analysis, and the societal impact of digital technologies. Directs the Center for Information Technology Policy.

faculty.name: Olga Troyanskaya
faculty.title: Maduraperuma/Khot Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Leader in computational biology and bioinformatics. Research integrates heterogeneous genomic data using ML to understand cellular processes and human disease. Director of Princeton Precision Health.

faculty.name: Michael Freedman
faculty.title: Robert E. Kahn Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Robert E. Kahn Professor and leader in distributed systems, networking, and security. Co-founder of TimescaleDB. Research in cloud infrastructure, programmable networks, and geo-distributed systems.

faculty.name: Elad Hazan
faculty.title: Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Leader in online learning and optimization for ML. Author of the definitive textbook on online convex optimization. Research in control theory for robotics, no-regret methods, and learning in dynamical systems.

faculty.name: Ryan Adams
faculty.title: Professor of Computer Science; Associate Chair
faculty.summary: Associate Chair and leader in Bayesian ML and probabilistic methods. Creator of widely-used Bayesian optimization tools. Research in automated experiment design and ML for scientific discovery.

faculty.name: Jia Deng
faculty.title: Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Leading computer vision researcher. Co-creator of the ImageNet dataset that sparked the deep learning revolution. Research spans visual recognition, 3D understanding, and ML for visual reasoning.

faculty.name: Karthik Narasimhan
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Leading researcher in NLP and AI agents. Research in language-guided reinforcement learning, interactive NLP, and building autonomous agents that use language to solve sequential decision-making tasks.

faculty.name: Danqi Chen
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Rising star in NLP. Creator of influential reading comprehension models and question-answering systems. Research in efficient LLM training, retrieval-augmented generation, and structured prediction.

faculty.name: Kyle Jamieson
faculty.title: Professor of Computer Science; Director of Graduate Studies
faculty.summary: Director of Graduate Studies and leader in wireless networking and mobile systems. Research in next-generation wireless protocols, IoT networking, and ML-driven network optimization. ACM Fellow.

faculty.name: Wyatt Lloyd
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Computer Science; Associate Chair
faculty.summary: Associate Chair and leader in distributed systems and storage. Research in consistency models, geo-replication, and building reliable large-scale distributed data stores.

faculty.name: Mark Braverman
faculty.title: Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Leading theoretical computer scientist. Research in interactive information theory, communication complexity, game theory, and algorithmic economics. Invited speaker at ICM 2014 and 2022.

faculty.name: Ran Raz
faculty.title: Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Leading complexity theorist. Contributions to circuit lower bounds, the parallel repetition theorem, and the quantum-classical complexity separation. Invited speaker at ICM.

faculty.name: Gillat Kol
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Rising theory researcher. Contributions to interactive compression, information complexity, and the parallel repetition theorem. Sloan Fellow and Packard Fellow.

faculty.name: Olga Russakovsky
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Leader in computer vision and AI fairness. Co-founder of the AI4ALL nonprofit for increasing diversity in AI. Research in visual recognition, algorithmic bias, and human-in-the-loop vision systems.

faculty.name: Tri Dao
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Rising star bridging ML and systems. Creator of FlashAttention, a widely-used algorithm for efficient transformer training (over 5K citations). Research in efficient ML algorithms and hardware-aware training.

faculty.name: Ben Raphael
faculty.title: Graduate Class of 1991 Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Leader in computational biology. Research in algorithm development for cancer genomics, analyzing tumor heterogeneity, and evolutionary reconstruction from genomic data.

faculty.name: Zeev Dvir
faculty.title: Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Leading complexity theorist and combinatorics researcher. Contributions to polynomial identity testing, locally decodable codes, and the Kakeya conjecture over finite fields.

faculty.name: Jonathan Mayer
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Computer Science and Public Affairs
