faculty.name: Tapti Palit
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research at the intersection of program analysis, software security, and systems. Automates translation of C/C++ code to memory-safe Rust, develops pointer analysis for CFI hardening, and builds tools for data protection and system call filtering. Joined Fall 2024.

faculty.name: Aditya V. Thakur
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Bridges formal methods with machine learning. Researches provable repair of deep neural networks with formal guarantees, automated ML infusion for HPC applications, and scalable program analysis tools. NSF CAREER and DOE Early Career Award recipient.

requirements.admission_model: Priority deadline Jan 15, submissions until Jan 31

faculty.name: Syed Jafar
faculty.title: Chancellor's Professor
faculty.summary: Leading researcher in network information theory. Known for seminal contributions to interference alignment and degrees of freedom in wireless networks. IEEE Information Theory Society Paper Award recipient.

faculty.name: Hamid Jafarkhani
faculty.title: Distinguished Professor
faculty.summary: Pioneer in space-time coding for multi-antenna wireless communications. Known for super-orthogonal space-time trellis codes and contributions to MIMO communication theory.

faculty.name: A. Lee Swindlehurst
faculty.title: Professor (Nicolaos G. and Sue Curtis Alexopoulos Presidential Chair, Department Chair)
faculty.summary: Department Chair. Research in signal processing for wireless communications, array processing, and MIMO radar systems. Known for contributions to signal processing for multiple-antenna systems.

faculty.name: Payam Heydari
faculty.title: Chancellor's Professor (Henry Samueli Faculty Excellence Professor)
faculty.summary: Research in high-frequency integrated circuit design for communication and sensing applications. Focus on silicon-based mm-wave and terahertz circuits and systems.

faculty.name: Jean-Luc Gaudiot
faculty.title: Distinguished Professor
faculty.summary: Research in computer architecture and parallel processing. Contributions to multithreaded processors, dataflow architectures, and instruction-level parallelism. IEEE Fellow and former Computer Society president.

faculty.name: Fadi Kurdahi
faculty.title: Distinguished Professor
faculty.summary: Research in VLSI system design and design automation. Focus on system-on-chip architectures, embedded and cyber-physical systems, and reconfigurable computing. IEEE Fellow.

faculty.name: Nader Bagherzadeh
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in computer architecture, parallel processing, and 3D integrated circuit design. Focus on GPU architectures, networks-on-chip, and efficient parallel computing platforms.

faculty.name: Pramod Khargonekar
faculty.title: Distinguished Professor
faculty.summary: Leading researcher in control systems theory. Known for foundational work in robust control and H-infinity control. Recent focus on control and optimization of power grids and renewable energy systems.

faculty.name: Ozdal Boyraz
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in integrated photonics and optical communications. Focus on silicon photonic devices, optical interconnects, and nonlinear optical signal processing for communications and sensing.

faculty.name: Peter Burke
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in nanoelectronic devices and their biomedical applications. Focus on carbon nanotube and graphene electronics for sensing and biomedical diagnostics.

faculty.name: Mohammad Al Faruque
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Director of the Center for Resilient Autonomous Systems. Research in cyber-physical systems and IoT with applications to smart grid, autonomous vehicles, and Industry 4.0.

faculty.name: Filippo Capolino
faculty.title: Dean's Professor
faculty.summary: Research in electromagnetics and photonics, including metamaterials, plasmonics, and antenna design. Focus on engineered electromagnetic structures for communications, sensing, and energy.

faculty.name: Aparna Chandramowlishwaran
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in high-performance computing and parallel algorithms for scientific computing applications. Focus on optimizing computational workflows for modern heterogeneous architectures.

faculty.name: Hung Cao
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research in micro-electromechanical systems (MEMS) for biomedical applications. Focus on implantable sensors, wearable health monitoring devices, and wireless biomedical systems.

faculty.name: Yanning Shen
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.sub_field: Machine Learning, Signal Processing, Network Science
faculty.summary: Research in machine learning and statistical signal processing for network data. Focus on graph-based learning, high-dimensional statistics, and applications to neuroscience and social networks.

faculty.name: Yasser Shoukry
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research in security and resilience of cyber-physical and autonomous systems. Focus on formal methods for detection and mitigation of attacks on autonomous vehicles and industrial control systems.

faculty.name: Marco Levorato
faculty.title: Professor (Joint CS and EECS)
faculty.summary: Research in wireless communications, edge computing, and machine learning for networked systems. Focus on AI-driven optimization of distributed IoT and edge systems.

faculty.name: Boris Vaisband
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research in VLSI circuits and systems. Focus on power delivery and clock distribution in 3D integrated systems and heterogeneous integration for high-performance computing.

faculty.name: Zhiying Wang
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research in coding theory and information theory. Contributions to coding for distributed storage systems, flash memories, and emerging storage technologies including DNA data storage.

faculty.name: Athina Markopoulou
faculty.title: Professor (EECS, CS)
faculty.summary: Director of the ProperData Center for data transparency and privacy. Research in network measurement, online privacy, and applying data science to understand network behavior.

requirements.admission_model: Applications open early October, close Dec 15

faculty.name: Pierre F. Baldi
faculty.title: Distinguished Professor
faculty.summary: Pioneer in deep learning and bioinformatics. Research spans neural networks, graphical models, and their applications to molecular biology, drug discovery, and cheminformatics.

faculty.name: Rina Dechter
faculty.title: Distinguished Professor (Associate Dean for Research)
faculty.summary: Leading researcher in automated reasoning and constraint processing. Known for foundational work on constraint satisfaction, AND/OR search, and Bayesian networks reasoning. Fellow of AAAI and ACM.

faculty.name: David Eppstein
faculty.title: Distinguished Professor
faculty.summary: Prolific researcher in algorithms and computational geometry. Known for contributions to graph algorithms, graph drawing, computational topology, and recreational mathematics. ACM Fellow.

faculty.name: Michael T. Goodrich
faculty.title: Distinguished Professor
faculty.summary: Research in computer security and algorithm design. Known for work on security-conscious algorithm design, authenticated data structures, and geometric computing. Author of widely-used algorithms textbooks.

faculty.name: Padhraic Smyth
faculty.title: Chancellor's Professor
faculty.summary: Research in machine learning, data mining, and statistical pattern recognition. Known for contributions to probabilistic modeling, time series analysis, and applied ML in science and environmental applications.

faculty.name: Charless Fowlkes
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in computer vision and machine learning, focusing on visual recognition, image segmentation, and their applications to biological image analysis and phenotyping.

faculty.name: Sameer Singh
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in machine learning and NLP. Focus on understanding and improving large language models, including robustness to distribution shift, interpretability, and evaluation methodologies.

faculty.name: Erik B. Sudderth
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in probabilistic machine learning and computer vision. Focus on Bayesian methods, graphical models, and deep generative models for visual scene understanding.

faculty.name: Sven Koenig
faculty.title: Chancellor's Professor and Bren Chair
faculty.summary: Research in AI planning and robotics. Known for contributions to heuristic search, multi-agent path finding, and combined task-and-motion planning for autonomous systems. Joined UCI July 2024.

faculty.name: Alexander Ihler
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in probabilistic graphical models and machine learning, including approximate inference algorithms, belief propagation, and applications to distributed sensor networks and deep learning.

faculty.name: Stephan Mandt
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research in probabilistic machine learning and deep generative models. Focus on variational autoencoders, Bayesian neural networks, and ML for scientific applications including climate and physics.

faculty.name: Michael Franz
faculty.title: Distinguished Professor
faculty.summary: Research in compilers, virtual machines, and software security. Pioneer in trace-based JIT compilation technology (used in browsers and mobile). Focus on moving target defenses and code diversity.

faculty.name: Nikil D. Dutt
faculty.title: Distinguished Professor
faculty.summary: Research in embedded systems, computer architecture, and electronic design automation. Focus on brain-inspired architectures, cyber-physical systems, and AI accelerators for edge devices.

faculty.name: Scott Mahlke
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in compiler technology and computer architecture, focusing on custom-fit architectures for energy-efficient and reliable computing from mobile to data center scale systems.

faculty.name: Stanislaw Jarecki
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in cryptography, including password-authenticated key exchange, threshold cryptography, and two-party secure computation protocols. Known for efficient cryptographic protocol design.

faculty.name: Nalini Venkatasubramanian
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in distributed and multimedia computing systems with focus on Internet of Things, smart cities, and emergency response systems. Director of the OC Data Science Initiative.

faculty.name: Vijay Vazirani
faculty.title: Distinguished Professor
faculty.summary: Leading theoretical computer scientist. Author of the definitive approximation algorithms textbook. Known for foundational work on approximation algorithms, matching theory, and algorithmic game theory.

faculty.name: Gene Tsudik
faculty.title: Distinguished Professor
faculty.summary: Research in computer and network security with emphasis on IoT security, privacy-enhancing technologies, and applied cryptography. Known for contributions to secure group communication.

faculty.name: Sangeetha Abdu Jyothi
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research in computer networking and systems, with recent work on AI/ML. Known for the award-winning study on solar superstorms' impact on Internet infrastructure. Focus on reliable networked systems.

faculty.name: Roy Fox
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research in reinforcement learning theory and applications, including skill discovery, representation learning, and RL for robotics. Focus on sample-efficient and explainable RL algorithms.

faculty.name: Paul Dourish
faculty.title: Chancellor's Professor
faculty.summary: Leading figure in HCI and CSCW. Research on the social and cultural dimensions of computing, embodied interaction, and the relationship between technology and everyday practice. ACM Fellow.

faculty.name: Gillian Hayes
faculty.title: Professor (Robert A. and Barbara L. Kleist Professor)
faculty.summary: Research in human-computer interaction with focus on health and education. Director of the STAR Lab, developing technologies for chronic disease management, autism interventions, and educational support.

faculty.name: Andre van der Hoek
faculty.title: Professor (Associate Dean of Academic Affairs)
faculty.summary: Research in software design and collaboration. Investigates how developers design software, fostering creative design through collaborative tools and methodologies. Head of the Software Design and Collaboration Lab.

faculty.name: Yunan Chen
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in health informatics and human-computer interaction. Focus on designing and evaluating clinical information systems to improve healthcare delivery, patient safety, and clinical workflow.

faculty.name: Bonnie Ruberg
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research in game studies and digital culture, with focus on gender and sexuality in games and interactive media. Known for work on queer game studies and LGBTQ representation in digital media.

faculty.name: Daniel Epstein
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research in human-computer interaction, personal informatics, and ubiquitous computing. Focus on self-tracking technologies, health behavior change, and designing for everyday reflection through data.

faculty.name: Stacy Branham
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research in accessibility and human-computer interaction. Focus on designing technologies with and for people with disabilities, particularly for blind and visually impaired communities.

faculty.name: Roderic N. Crooks
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research at the intersection of science and technology studies, critical data studies, and HCI. Focus on data-driven technologies in education, digital equity, and algorithmic justice.

faculty.name: Thomas Zimmermann
faculty.title: Chancellor's Professor and Bren Chair
faculty.summary: Research in software engineering and data science. Known for contributions to mining software repositories, empirical software engineering, and developer productivity. Joined UCI January 2025 from Microsoft Research.

faculty.name: Magda El Zarki
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in networking, wireless communication, and multimedia systems. Director of the Virtual Reality Lab, exploring VR/AR technologies for education and healthcare applications.

faculty.name: Joshua Garcia
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research in automated software analysis, combining program analysis and machine learning to improve software quality. Focus on testing autonomous driving systems and managing software vulnerabilities.

faculty.name: Iftekhar Ahmed
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research at the intersection of software testing, static analysis, and machine learning. Focus on empirical studies and tools to improve software quality in real-world development environments.

faculty.name: Matthew Bietz
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Teaching
faculty.summary: Research in human-computer interaction and CSCW with focus on health and biomedical applications. Studying how technology supports collaborative scientific research and healthcare delivery. Joined UCI July 2024.

faculty.name: Aditi Majumder
faculty.title: Professor (CS)
faculty.summary: Research in computer graphics, visualization, and novel display technologies. Focus on multi-projector displays, ubiquitous computing interfaces, and interactive visualization systems.

faculty.name: Gopi Meenakshisundaram
faculty.title: Professor (CS)
faculty.summary: Research in computer graphics and virtual reality. Focus on geometry processing, image-based rendering, and applications of graphics in human-computer interaction and VR systems.

faculty.name: Sam Malek
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in software engineering with focus on software analysis and testing. Director of the SEAL Lab. Noted for work on software architecture, mobile app analysis, and security for software supply chains.

faculty.name: Mark S. Ackerman
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in CSCW and HCI. Pioneer in understanding how people share information and expertise in collaborative environments. Studies the social-technical gap in collaborative systems design.

faculty.name: Richard N. Taylor
faculty.title: Professor Emeritus (Director, Institute for Software Research)
faculty.summary: Founding figure of the software engineering program at UCI. Pioneer in software architecture research and the founding director of the Institute for Software Research (ISR). Known for event-based and REST architectural styles.

faculty.name: Andre van der Hoek
faculty.title: Professor (Associate Dean of Academic Affairs)
faculty.summary: Research in software design and creative collaboration. Leads the Software Design and Collaboration Lab (SDCL). Focus on understanding and improving how software developers design and collaborate on complex systems.

faculty.name: Sam Malek
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Director of the Software Engineering and Analysis Laboratory (SEAL). Research in automated software analysis, testing, and security. Focus on mobile and web application analysis and software supply chain security.

faculty.name: Brian Demsky
faculty.title: Professor (CS)
faculty.summary: Research in software reliability, program analysis, and compilers. Focus on automated bug detection, safe parallelism, and building reliable concurrent software systems.

faculty.name: Thomas Zimmermann
faculty.title: Chancellor's Professor and Bren Chair
faculty.summary: World-leading researcher in empirical software engineering and mining software repositories. Joined UCI from Microsoft Research where he led the Productivity and Intelligence group. Joined January 2025.

faculty.name: Joshua Garcia
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.sub_field: Software Engineering, Program Analysis, ML for SE
faculty.summary: Research in automated software analysis combining static/dynamic program analysis with machine learning. Applications to autonomous driving system testing, vulnerability management, and architecture analysis.

faculty.name: Iftekhar Ahmed
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research in software quality assurance, combining testing, static analysis, and machine learning to detect and fix defects in real-world software systems. Focus on empirical validation of software quality techniques.

faculty.name: Cristina Lopes
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in programming languages and software engineering. Known for work on aspect-oriented programming, software naturalness, and empirical studies of programming language usage in open-source ecosystems.

faculty.name: James Jones
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in software debugging, fault localization, and software comprehension. Developing automated techniques to help developers understand, test, and debug software systems more efficiently.

faculty.name: Michael Franz
faculty.title: Distinguished Professor (CS)
faculty.summary: Research in compilers and software security. Pioneer of trace-based JIT compilation. Current focus on compiler-generated software diversity for security. Fellow of ACM, IEEE, AAAS.

faculty.name: Alexandru Nicolau
faculty.title: Professor (CS)
faculty.summary: Research in compilers, parallel programming, and code optimization. Known for contributions to instruction-level parallelism, speculative execution, and compiler-directed parallelization for multicore architectures.

faculty.name: Scott Mahlke
faculty.title: Professor (CS)
faculty.summary: Research in compilers and architecture, focusing on generating efficient code for custom-fit architectures. Applications in mobile computing, data centers, and embedded systems.

faculty.name: Debra J. Richardson
faculty.title: Professor Emeritus
faculty.summary: Pioneer in software testing and validation research. Contributions to specification-based testing methods, software process improvement, and interdisciplinary applications of software engineering.

faculty.name: Alexander Veidenbaum
faculty.title: Professor (CS)
faculty.summary: Research in computer architecture and compilers with applications to embedded systems. Focus on compiler-architecture co-design, efficient code generation for specialized processors, and power-aware compilation.

faculty.name: Mark S. Ackerman
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in CSCW with applications to software engineering. Investigates how developers share knowledge and expertise in distributed collaborative environments, informing better engineering tool design.

faculty.name: Ian G. Harris
faculty.title: Professor (CS)
faculty.summary: Research in hardware/software co-validation and testing. Focus on verification approaches that span the hardware-software interface, with applications to IoT and embedded system security.

faculty.name: Nalini Venkatasubramanian
faculty.title: Professor (CS)
faculty.summary: Research in distributed and multimedia computing with focus on middleware architectures and quality-of-service. Director of the Resilient and Sustainable Communities Research Center.

faculty.name: Adnan Darwiche
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in automated reasoning, knowledge representation, and probabilistic graphical models. Known for work on symbolic reasoning, tractable inference, and explainable AI.

faculty.name: Amit Sahai
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Leading researcher in theoretical cryptography, including attribute-based encryption, functional encryption, and program obfuscation. Director of the Center for Encrypted Functionalities.

faculty.name: Guy Van den Broeck
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research on tractable probabilistic models, combining probabilistic reasoning with deep learning. Leading the Statistical and Relational AI (StarAI) Lab.

faculty.name: Songwu Lu
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research on wireless networking, mobile systems, and network security. Focus on 5G/next-G cellular networks, software-defined networking, and cloud computing.

faculty.name: Mani Srivastava
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in networked embedded systems, cyber-physical systems, IoT, and mobile health. Leading the Networked and Embedded Systems Lab (NESL).

faculty.name: Jason Cong
faculty.title: Professor (Volgenau Professor of Engineering Excellence)
faculty.summary: Pioneer in electronic design automation, FPGA synthesis, and customizable computing. Director of the Center for Domain-Specific Computing (CDSC).

faculty.name: Miryung Kim
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in software engineering, program analysis, and software evolution. Focus on code refactoring, API migration, and large-scale software analytics.

faculty.name: Yizhou Sun
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research on mining large-scale information networks, graph neural networks, and knowledge graph reasoning. Leading the Data Mining Lab.

faculty.name: Wei Wang
faculty.title: Professor (Leonard Kleinrock Term Professor of Computer Science)
faculty.summary: Research in data mining, machine learning, and computational biology. Focus on genomics, epigenomics, and integrative analysis of biological data.

faculty.name: Quanquan Gu
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research in machine learning foundations, optimization for deep learning, and reinforcement learning theory. Focus on understanding deep learning from an optimization perspective.

faculty.name: Cho-Jui Hsieh
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research in large-scale machine learning, optimization algorithms, and NLP. Focus on scalable training, adversarial robustness, and efficient deep learning.

faculty.name: Bolei Zhou
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research in computer vision, interpretable machine learning, and AI for autonomous driving. Known for work on interpretable deep networks and scene understanding.

faculty.name: Kai-Wei Chang
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research in natural language processing, focusing on robust and fair NLP models, multilingual representations, and knowledge-grounded language models.

faculty.name: Sriram Sankararaman
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research at the intersection of computer science and human genetics. Focus on statistical methods for analyzing genomic data and understanding complex traits.

faculty.name: Jason Ernst
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in computational epigenomics and regulatory genomics. Known for developing ChromHMM for chromatin state discovery and large-scale epigenomic data analysis.

faculty.name: Eleazar Eskin
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Chair of Computational Medicine. Research in statistical genetics, bioinformatics, and computational biology. Focus on GWAS, eQTL analysis, and population genetics methods.

faculty.name: George Varghese
faculty.title: Professor (Jonathan B. Postel Professor of Networking)
faculty.summary: Pioneer in network algorithmics and high-speed packet processing. Known for foundational work on IP lookup, packet classification, and self-stabilizing protocols.

faculty.name: Raghu Meka
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in theoretical computer science, particularly pseudorandomness, computational complexity, and learning theory. Known for work on derandomization and Boolean function analysis.

faculty.name: Aditya Grover
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research in probabilistic machine learning, generative models, and AI for scientific discovery. Focus on foundation models for physical sciences and sequential decision-making.

faculty.name: Saadia Gabriel
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research in NLP with focus on fairness, ethics, and social implications of AI. Working on detecting misinformation, understanding social biases in language models.

faculty.name: Eleazar Eskin
faculty.title: Professor (Chair, Computational Medicine)
faculty.summary: Founding Chair of Computational Medicine Department. Research in statistical genetics, human genetics, and computational methods for analyzing genomic variation. Co-directs the undergraduate bioinformatics minor.

faculty.name: Matteo Pellegrini
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Director of the UCLA-DOE Institute for Genomics and Proteomics. Research on computational analysis of genomic and epigenomic data, particularly DNA methylation and its role in development and disease.

faculty.name: Jason Ernst
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Developer of ChromHMM for chromatin state discovery. Research in computational epigenomics, regulatory genomics, and machine learning methods for analyzing large-scale functional genomics data. Joint appointment in Computer Science.

faculty.name: Jingyi Jessica Li
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in statistical genomics and bioinformatics, developing methods for analyzing single-cell and bulk RNA-seq data, and statistical methodology for genomic studies. Joint appointment in Statistics & Data Science, Human Genetics, Computational Medicine, and Biostatistics.

faculty.name: Bogdan Pasaniuc
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in statistical genetics and computational genomics, focusing on methods for analyzing genetic variation in diverse populations, fine-mapping GWAS loci, and polygenic risk prediction.

faculty.name: Xia Yang
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in systems biology and integrative genomics, combining multi-omics data with network modeling to understand complex diseases including metabolic disorders and cardiovascular disease.

faculty.name: Xinshu Grace Xiao
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research on computational analysis of post-transcriptional regulation, including alternative splicing and RNA editing. Integrating RNA biology with genomic data for disease understanding.

faculty.name: Roy Wollman
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in quantitative systems biology, combining single-cell measurements with computational modeling to understand cellular signaling and gene expression dynamics.

faculty.name: Alexander Hoffmann
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in systems biology of cellular signaling, using computational modeling and quantitative experiments to understand immune signaling pathways, particularly NF-kB dynamics.

faculty.name: Eric Deeds
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research on understanding how molecular interactions give rise to cellular behavior, focusing on protein interaction networks, biochemical signaling, and the evolution of protein complexes.

faculty.name: Paul Boutros
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Director of the Jonsson Comprehensive Cancer Center Cancer Data Science program. Research in cancer genomics, developing computational methods for analyzing tumor evolution, heterogeneity, and biomarkers.

faculty.name: Thomas Graeber
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in cancer systems biology using multi-omics approaches (proteomics, metabolomics, genomics) to study cancer signaling, drug response mechanisms, and therapy resistance.

faculty.name: Aaron Meyer
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research at the intersection of systems biology and immunology, developing computational methods to study cell signaling and immune responses, with applications to cancer immunotherapy.

faculty.name: Harold Pimentel
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research in statistical methods for functional genomics, including RNA-seq analysis, gene regulatory network inference, and understanding genetic variation effects on gene expression.

faculty.name: Brunilda Balliu
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research in statistical genetics, focusing on understanding how genetic variation affects molecular traits at the single-cell level and developing methods for single-cell eQTL analysis.

faculty.name: Nandita Garud
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research in population genetics and evolutionary genomics, with focus on detecting natural selection in genomes, understanding the evolutionary dynamics of the gut microbiome.

faculty.name: Van Savage
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in computational and mathematical biology, developing theoretical models for biological scaling laws, metabolic networks, and ecological systems dynamics.

faculty.name: Shantanu Joshi
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research in computational imaging and biomedical informatics, applying computer vision and machine learning to neuroimaging analysis and brain mapping applications.

faculty.name: Kirk Lohmueller
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Home Area Director of the Bioinformatics program. Research in population genetics and evolutionary biology, using computational methods to understand genetic variation and evolutionary processes.

faculty.name: James Lloyd-Smith
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in mathematical and computational modeling of infectious disease dynamics, zoonotic disease emergence, and ecological drivers of disease transmission.

requirements.admission_model: Reviewed by CSE Admissions Committee (department admit)

funding.note: PhD students typically supported via TA/GSR/fellowships (verify guarantee)

faculty.name: Steven Swanson
faculty.title: Professor (Department Chair)
faculty.summary: Research in computer architecture and systems, focusing on non-volatile memory technologies, flash storage systems, and hardware-software co-design for emerging memory hierarchies.

faculty.name: Russell Impagliazzo
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Leading theoretical computer scientist. Known for foundational work on pseudorandomness, derandomization, and the celebrated five worlds of average-case complexity. Research spans circuit complexity, cryptography, and hardness of approximation.

faculty.name: Mihir Bellare
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Leading cryptographer with foundational contributions to provable security. Known for the random oracle model, BLS signatures, HMAC, and authenticated encryption standards. Multiple test-of-time awards.

faculty.name: Stefan Savage
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Pioneer in empirical security research measuring large-scale cybercrime ecosystems, including spam, phishing, and automotive security. MacArthur Fellow. Research on practical security and measurement.

faculty.name: Hovav Shacham
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in computer security and applied cryptography. Known for contributions to memory safety, proof of retrievability, and analysis of blockchain security and decentralized systems.

faculty.name: Ranjit Jhala
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in programming languages and formal methods. Creator of LiquidHaskell and the Liquid Types framework. Focus on automated program verification using refinement types.

faculty.name: Ravi Ramamoorthi
faculty.title: Distinguished Professor
faculty.summary: Pioneer in computer graphics and computational imaging. Research on realistic rendering, image-based lighting, computational photography, and the mathematics of light transport.

faculty.name: Pavel Pevzner
faculty.title: Professor (Ronald R. Taylor Chair)
faculty.summary: Leading computational biologist. Pioneer in de novo peptide sequencing, genome assembly algorithms, and mass spectrometry analysis. Known for founding the field of computational proteomics.

faculty.name: Julian McAuley
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in machine learning for recommender systems, personalization, and NLP. Creator of widely-used benchmark datasets for product recommendation and sentiment analysis.

faculty.name: Rose Yu
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research in machine learning for spatiotemporal data and dynamical systems. Focus on physics-informed deep learning, traffic forecasting, and scientific ML applications.

faculty.name: Kamalika Chaudhuri
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in machine learning theory with focus on privacy-preserving learning, fairness in ML, and robustness. Known for contributions to differentially private learning algorithms.

faculty.name: Hao Su
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in computer vision, 3D deep learning, and embodied AI. Focus on 3D scene understanding, robotic manipulation, and simulation-to-real transfer for robot learning.

faculty.name: Laurel Riek
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in human-robot interaction and healthcare robotics. Director of the Healthcare Robotics Lab, developing robots that assist people with disabilities and healthcare providers.

faculty.name: Shachar Lovett
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in theoretical computer science at the interface of complexity theory, additive combinatorics, and algebraic methods. Contributions to pseudorandomness and arithmetic complexity.

faculty.name: Alex C. Snoeren
faculty.title: Professor (Co-Vice Chair for Academic Personnel)
faculty.summary: Research in computer networks and systems, focusing on data center networking, wide-area measurement, and cloud infrastructure. Co-Vice Chair of the department.

faculty.name: Yiying Zhang
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research in computer systems, particularly distributed storage and database systems. Focus on high-performance and fault-tolerant storage architectures for modern data centers.

faculty.name: Nadia Polikarpova
faculty.title: Professor (Vice Chair for Graduate Education)
faculty.summary: Research in programming languages and program synthesis. Focus on type-driven program synthesis enabling programmers to specify intent via types and generate provably correct implementations.

faculty.name: Sanjoy Dasgupta
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in machine learning algorithms, particularly clustering, unsupervised learning, and active learning. Author of a widely-used textbook on machine learning. Known for theoretical analysis of clustering algorithms.

faculty.name: Dean M. Tullsen
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Pioneer in computer architecture, known for inventing simultaneous multithreading (SMT/Hyper-Threading). Research on processor microarchitecture, architectural support for security, and hardware acceleration.

faculty.name: Taylor Berg-Kirkpatrick
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research in natural language processing and machine learning, focusing on unsupervised and semi-supervised methods for text, document layout analysis, and historical document processing.

faculty.name: Pavel Pevzner
faculty.title: Professor (Ronald R. Taylor Chair, Program Co-Director)
faculty.summary: Co-Director of the BISB program. Pioneer in computational molecular biology. Foundational work on de novo peptide sequencing, genome assembly algorithms, and computational mass spectrometry.

faculty.name: Terry Gaasterland
faculty.title: Professor (Program Director)
faculty.summary: Program Director of BISB. Research in computational genome analysis, including comparative genomics, transcriptome analysis, and methods for identifying causal genetic variants.

faculty.name: Vineet Bafna
faculty.title: Professor (CSE)
faculty.summary: Research in bioinformatics algorithms with focus on structural variation detection in cancer genomes, circulating tumor DNA analysis, and algorithmic problems in protein mass spectrometry.

faculty.name: Trey Ideker
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Pioneer in biological network analysis. Research on integrating multi-omics data with protein interaction networks to understand disease mechanisms, particularly cancer. Creator of Cytoscape network analysis tools.

faculty.name: Hannah Carter
faculty.title: Professor (Program Associate Director)
faculty.summary: Associate Director of BISB. Research in cancer genomics, developing computational methods for interpreting genetic variants in tumors and understanding their functional impact through network-based approaches.

faculty.name: Jill Mesirov
faculty.title: Professor (Associate Vice Chancellor, Computational Health Sciences)
faculty.summary: Research in computational biology and cancer genomics. Known for developing Gene Set Enrichment Analysis (GSEA) and influential bioinformatics tools. Focus on integrative analysis of cancer genomic data.

faculty.name: Rob Knight
faculty.title: Professor (Pediatrics, CSE, BioE)
faculty.summary: Leading microbiome researcher. Pioneer in developing computational methods for analyzing microbial communities. Co-founder of the American Gut Project and QIIME bioinformatics platform for microbiome analysis.

faculty.name: Shankar Subramaniam
faculty.title: Professor (Bioengineering, CSE)
faculty.summary: Founder of the Bioinformatics and Systems Biology graduate program. Research in systems biology linking genomics to metabolic and signaling networks, with applications to understanding disease mechanisms.

faculty.name: Bernhard Palsson
faculty.title: Professor (Y.C. Fung Endowed Chair in Bioengineering)
faculty.summary: Pioneer of genome-scale metabolic network reconstruction and constraint-based modeling. Research on computational methods for predicting cellular phenotypes from genome sequences.

faculty.name: Siavash Mirarab
faculty.title: Associate Professor (ECE)
faculty.summary: Research in computational phylogenetics, developing methods for scalable species tree estimation from genomic data. Known for ASTRAL species tree inference method.

faculty.name: Melissa Gymrek
faculty.title: Associate Professor (CS, Medicine)
faculty.summary: Research in computational genomics focusing on the role of short tandem repeats in human phenotypes and disease. Developing algorithms for genotyping and analyzing repetitive genetic variation.

faculty.name: Yatish Turakhia
faculty.title: Assistant Professor (ECE)
faculty.summary: Research on algorithms and hardware accelerators for genome analysis. Known for developing efficient algorithms for large-scale sequence analysis and phylogenomic inference.

faculty.name: Kun Zhang
faculty.title: Professor (Bioengineering)
faculty.summary: Pioneer in single-cell multi-omics technologies. Research on developing methods for analyzing single-cell genome, epigenome, and transcriptome data, with applications to cancer and developmental biology.

faculty.name: Nathan E. Lewis
faculty.title: Professor (Pediatrics)
faculty.summary: Research in systems biology using genome-scale metabolic models integrated with multi-omics data. Focus on understanding cellular metabolism, glycosylation networks, and biomanufacturing optimization.

faculty.name: Nuno Bandeira
faculty.title: Professor (Pharmacy, CSE)
faculty.summary: Research in computational mass spectrometry, developing algorithms and software tools for identifying proteins and their modifications from tandem mass spectrometry data.

faculty.name: Gene Yeo
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in RNA biology and genomics, developing computational and experimental methods to study RNA processing and RNA-binding proteins in neurodegeneration and autism.

faculty.name: Ludmil Alexandrov
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research on mutational processes in cancer. Known for discovering cancer mutational signatures and developing computational methods to characterize the processes that generate somatic mutations.

faculty.name: Vikas Bansal
faculty.title: Associate Professor (Pediatrics, CSE)
faculty.summary: Research in computational genomics, developing algorithms for haplotype phasing, variant calling, and analyzing structural variation in genomes. Focus on applications in rare disease diagnosis.

faculty.name: Tiffany Amariuta
faculty.title: Assistant Professor (HDSI, BMI)
faculty.summary: Research in statistical genetics, developing methods for fine-mapping disease-associated genetic variants and understanding the genetic architecture of complex traits across diverse populations.

faculty.name: Kit Curtius
faculty.title: Assistant Professor (Biomedical Informatics)
faculty.summary: Research in mathematical modeling of cancer evolution. Focus on developing computational approaches to optimize cancer screening strategies and understand progression dynamics using genomic data.

requirements.admission_model: Direct PhD entry allowed without MS

funding.note: PhD students typically funded via fellowships/GSR/TA; first-year NRST covered for nonresidents

faculty.name: Divyakant Agrawal
faculty.title: Distinguished Professor (Chair)
faculty.summary: Department Chair. Research in database systems and distributed data management. Focus on scalable data processing in cloud environments and big data analytics. ACM Distinguished Scientist.

faculty.name: Amr El Abbadi
faculty.title: Distinguished Professor
faculty.summary: Research in data management for distributed and cloud systems. Focus on fault-tolerant distributed databases, scalable data processing, and recently blockchain data management. ACM Fellow.

faculty.name: Timothy Sherwood
faculty.title: Distinguished Professor
faculty.summary: Research in computer architecture, particularly architectural support for security. Known for work on timing channel analysis, hardware security, and designing novel microarchitectures for emerging workloads.

faculty.name: Linda Petzold
faculty.title: Distinguished Professor (Mehrabian Engineering Chair)
faculty.summary: Pioneer in numerical methods for differential-algebraic equations and scientific computing. Research in computational methods for systems biology, particularly stochastic simulation of cellular processes. NAS member, NAE member.

faculty.name: Giovanni Vigna
faculty.title: Distinguished Professor
faculty.summary: Leading researcher in computer security. Known for work on malware analysis, web security, and vulnerability detection. Director of the UCSB SecLab and co-founder of the Shellphish hacking team. ACM Fellow.

faculty.name: Ambuj K. Singh
faculty.title: Distinguished Professor
faculty.sub_field: Data Mining, Network Science, Machine Learning
faculty.summary: Research in data mining, network science, and graph-based machine learning. Focus on analyzing complex networks in biology, social media, and neuroscience. Leads the Dynamo lab.

faculty.name: Subhash Suri
faculty.title: Distinguished Professor
faculty.summary: Research in algorithms with contributions to computational geometry, network algorithms, and data structures. Recent work on privacy-preserving algorithms and algorithmic game theory.

faculty.name: Tobias Hollerer
faculty.title: Distinguished Professor
faculty.summary: Pioneer in augmented reality research. Focus on AR/VR systems, spatial computing, and 3D user interfaces. Known for creating the Four Eyes Lab pushing the boundaries of mixed reality.

faculty.name: William Wang
faculty.title: Professor (Mellichamp Endowed Chair in Mind and Machine Intelligence)
faculty.summary: Research in natural language processing and machine learning. Focus on language understanding, knowledge-grounded dialogue, and integrating structured knowledge with neural language models. Leads the NLP Group.

faculty.name: Chandra Krintz
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in distributed systems, cloud computing, and IoT solutions for societal challenges. Leads the UCSB SmartFarm project applying computing to sustainable agriculture. ACM Distinguished Scientist.

faculty.name: Rich Wolski
faculty.title: Professor (Duval Family Presidential Chair in Energy Efficiency)
faculty.summary: Research in high-performance cloud computing and energy-efficient distributed systems. Known for foundational work on cloud resource management and the Eucalyptus open-source cloud platform.

faculty.name: Tevfik Bultan
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in software analysis and verification. Known for work on string analysis for web applications and comprehensive formal verification frameworks. Leads the VLab.

faculty.name: Xifeng Yan
faculty.title: Professor (Venkatesh Narayanamurti Endowed Chair in CS)
faculty.summary: Research in data mining and machine learning. Focus on graph mining, network analysis, and knowledge discovery from large-scale structured and textual data. Known for contributions to graph pattern mining.

faculty.name: Christopher Kruegel
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in computer security, particularly malware analysis and detection. Known for influential work on botnet analysis and web application security. Co-founder of Lastline (acquired by VMWare).

faculty.name: Elizabeth Belding
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in wireless networking and Information and Communication Technologies for Development (ICTD). Focus on bringing Internet connectivity to rural and underserved communities worldwide. ACM Fellow.

faculty.name: Daniel Lokshtanov
faculty.title: Professor (Vice Chair)
faculty.summary: Vice Chair. Research in theoretical computer science, particularly parameterized complexity and graph algorithms. Known for foundational contributions to kernelization theory and fixed-parameter tractability.

faculty.name: Eric Vigoda
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in theoretical computer science with focus on randomized algorithms, Markov chain Monte Carlo methods, and approximate counting. Known for analyzing phase transitions in combinatorial structures.

faculty.name: Ben Hardekopf
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in programming languages and compilers with focus on program analysis for security and optimization. Known for work on pointer analysis, information flow, and compiler-based security.

faculty.name: Yu Feng
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research in programming languages with focus on automated program analysis and verification. Developing tools for automatically finding bugs and verifying properties of real-world software systems.

faculty.name: Arpit Gupta
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research in computer networking with focus on network management, cloud infrastructure networking, and Internet measurement. Building practical systems for network operations.

faculty.name: Rebecca Willett
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research centers on machine learning, signal processing, and large-scale data science, spanning high-dimensional statistics, inverse problems, learning theory, and optimization. Applies these methods to astronomy, materials science, microscopy, and electronic health records.

faculty.name: Ben Y. Zhao
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Focuses on protecting human creatives from harms of generative AI. Has published over 190 articles spanning security and privacy, machine learning, networking, and HCI, with emphasis on security and privacy problems in ML algorithms and systems.

faculty.name: Heather Zheng
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research centers on copyright protection and fake content detection related to generative AI, security and privacy of deep learning systems including robustness against adversarial attacks, and mobile/IoT/VR sensing and its privacy and security implications.

faculty.name: Rick Stevens
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research spans high-performance computing architecture, bioinformatics, infectious disease, cancer modeling, and AI methods for scientific and biomedical challenges. Leads projects on deep learning frameworks for cancer including the CANDLE project.

faculty.name: Michael Maire
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research spans computer vision including perceptual organization and object recognition, and deep learning including neural network architectures and optimization.

faculty.name: Risi Kondor
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Focuses on foundational ML methods inspired by algebra and computational harmonic analysis. Works on graph neural networks for chemistry, molecular force field learning, neural network approximations of quantum states, and group equivariant neural networks.

faculty.name: Chenhao Tan
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Focuses on human-centered machine learning and NLP, aiming to enable effective human-AI interaction by analyzing how language shapes decisions, generating human-centered AI explanations, and developing new paradigms for human-AI collaboration.

faculty.name: Ce Zhang
faculty.title: Associate Professor

faculty.name: Haifeng Xu
faculty.title: Associate Professor

faculty.name: Ari Holtzman
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Works on dialogue systems and fundamental text generation research. Proposed Nucleus Sampling, a decoding algorithm used broadly in deployed systems such as the OpenAI API. Won the inaugural Amazon Alexa Prize in 2017.

faculty.name: Tian Li
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Focuses on distributed optimization, federated learning, and trustworthy ML. Designs learning algorithms that account for practical constraints related to accuracy, scalability, and trustworthiness.

faculty.name: Yuxin Chen
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research centers on probabilistic reasoning and machine learning, with a current focus on interactive machine learning systems involving active learning, sequential decision making, interpretable models, and machine teaching. Received NSF CAREER Award in 2026.

faculty.name: Mina Lee
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Works at the intersection of NLP and HCI, examining how AI systems shape human writing, reading, and thinking. Designs and evaluates AI writing tools and studies risks and opportunities in AI-assisted composition.

faculty.name: Nick Feamster
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Designs network protocols and systems to improve the Internet, applying empirical network measurement and machine learning to understand and improve network performance, security, and privacy. ACM Fellow.

faculty.name: Andrew A. Chien
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Works on cloud software and hardware, supercomputer architecture, and sustainable computing. Leads the Large-Scale Sustainable Systems group focusing on sustainable, scalable large-scale systems including cloud, HPC, edge, and mobile/IoT computing. Editor in Chief of Communications of the ACM.

faculty.name: Henry Hoffmann
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Leads the Self-aware computing group (SEEC project), focusing on adaptive techniques for managing power, energy, accuracy, and performance in computing systems. Applies control theory and machine learning to computer system management. PECASE awardee (2019).

faculty.name: Ian Foster
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Focuses on distributed, parallel, and data-intensive computing technologies applied to scientific problems in materials science, climate change, and biomedicine. Develops tools and techniques enabling high-performance computing for qualitatively new scientific discoveries.

faculty.name: Michael Franklin
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Focuses on large-scale data intelligence systems including massively parallel databases, federated data systems, and scalable data-centric AI systems. One of the original creators of Apache Spark. Founding Faculty Co-Director of the Data Science Institute.

faculty.name: Shan Lu
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research centers on software reliability and efficiency, focusing on detecting, diagnosing, and fixing functional and performance bugs in large software systems, with a special emphasis on concurrency bugs.

faculty.name: Haryadi Gunawi
faculty.title: Professor

faculty.name: Junchen Jiang
faculty.title: Associate Professor

faculty.name: László Babai
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Works in theoretical computer science and discrete mathematics, focusing on computational complexity, algorithms, combinatorics, and finite groups. Known for pioneering Las Vegas algorithms, interactive proofs, and holographic proofs.

faculty.name: Alexander A. Razborov
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Primary focus is complexity theory including circuit complexity, proof complexity, quantum computations, and communication complexity. Also actively explores discrete mathematics including extremal combinatorics.

faculty.name: William Fefferman
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Focuses on quantum computation and theoretical computer science, working at the intersection of CS with physics, statistics, and other mathematical sciences. Part of the EPiQC project on enabling practical-scale quantum computing.

faculty.name: Aaron Potechin
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research concentrates on the sum-of-squares hierarchy, a class of semidefinite programs that rank among the most potent tools for combinatorial optimization and approximation algorithms.

faculty.name: William Hoza
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Works in computational complexity theory, focusing on relationships between computational resources like time, memory, and randomness. Particularly interested in converting randomized algorithms into deterministic ones without substantially increasing memory usage.

faculty.summary: Professor of CS and Biomedical Informatics. Senior Advisor for Generative AI. Research on software engineering, generative AI, cybersecurity, and deep learning with applications from healthcare to manufacturing.

faculty.name: Thomas Beckers
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Assistant Professor of CS. Research on physics-enhanced learning, nonparametric models, and safe learning-based control, bridging machine learning with control theory.

faculty.name: Matthew Berger
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Assistant Professor of CS and Computer Engineering. Research at the intersection of data visualization and machine learning, using ML to enhance visual data exploration and visualization to understand ML models.

faculty.name: Catie Chang
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Assistant Professor of ECE, CS, and Biomedical Engineering. Research at the intersection of neuroscience and machine learning, focusing on brain imaging and neural signal processing.

faculty.name: Tyler Derr
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Assistant Professor of CS. NSF CAREER award winner. Research on network analysis for data with positive and negative interactions, graph neural networks, and social media analysis.

faculty.name: Yu Huang
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Assistant Professor of CS. Research on natural language processing, machine learning, and computational linguistics.

faculty.name: Yuankai Huo
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Assistant Professor of CS, ECE, and Pathology. Research on medical image computing using deep learning, focusing on safe and trustworthy AI for medical imaging analysis.

faculty.name: David Hyde
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Assistant Professor of CS. Leads the SOL Lab. NSF CAREER award winner. Research on algorithms at the intersection of simulation, optimization, and machine learning for physical intelligence.

faculty.name: Soheil Kolouri
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Assistant Professor of CS. Research on computer vision and machine learning, with focus on optimal transport, representation learning, and deep learning.

faculty.name: Forrest Laine
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Assistant Professor of Mechanical Engineering and CS. Research on computational game theory, robotics, multi-agent interaction, trajectory optimization, and robust machine learning for autonomous systems.

faculty.name: Kevin Leach
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Assistant Professor of CS. Director of Graduate Recruitment. Research on software security, systems, program analysis, and binary analysis.

faculty.name: Meiyi Ma
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Assistant Professor of CS. Research on integrating machine learning and formal methods for safe, robust, and reliable cyber-physical systems, with applications to smart cities.

faculty.name: Daniel Moyer
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.sub_field: Medical Image Analysis and Machine Learning
faculty.summary: Assistant Professor of CS. Director of VINE Lab. Research on machine learning for medical imaging including fetal MRI, neuroimaging, and multi-site image harmonization.

faculty.name: Bryan Ward
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Assistant Professor of CS and ECE. Research on security and resilience of real-time and embedded systems, including industrial control systems and space systems.

faculty.name: James Weimer
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Assistant Professor of CS and Biomedical Engineering. Research on learning-enabled cyber-physical systems, medical IoT, and addressing challenges of limited data and dataset shifts.

faculty.name: Jie Ying Wu
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Assistant Professor of CS, Biomedical Engineering, ECE, and Mechanical Engineering. Director of MAPLE Lab. Research on surgical robotics, ML for surgery, and real-time 3D scene understanding.

faculty.name: Josh F. Peterson
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.sub_field: Precision Medicine and Clinical Decision Support
faculty.summary: Interim Chair of DBMI and Director of the Center for Precision Medicine. Research on clinical decision support to improve drug safety and efficacy, translating genomic technologies into routine care. PI for eMERGE Network coordinating center and PREDICT pharmacogenomics program.

faculty.name: S. Trent Rosenbloom
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Vice Chair of Faculty Affairs. Professor of Biomedical Informatics, Internal Medicine, and Pediatrics. Research on patient engagement technologies, patient portals, patient-generated health data, and informatics evaluation.

faculty.name: Jessica S. Ancker
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.sub_field: Consumer Health Informatics and Human Factors
faculty.summary: Vice Chair for Educational Affairs. Professor of Biomedical Informatics. Research on health numeracy and literacy, human factors and behavioral economics in informatics, and health IT evaluation.

faculty.name: Bradley Malin
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.sub_field: Data Privacy and Machine Learning in Healthcare
faculty.summary: Accenture Professor of Biomedical Informatics, Biostatistics, and Computer Science. Vice Chair for Research Affairs. Co-director of ADVANCE Center. Research on big data management, health data privacy and security, machine learning over clinical databases, and genomic privacy. Elected to National Academy of Medicine.

faculty.name: Martin Were
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.sub_field: Global Health Informatics and Health Equity
faculty.summary: Vice Chair for Fair and Ethical Informatics. Professor of Biomedical Informatics and Medicine. Research on global health informatics, clinical decision support in resource-limited settings, and fair and ethical informatics.

faculty.name: Peter J. Embi
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.sub_field: Learning Health Systems and Translational Informatics
faculty.summary: Co-Director of ADVANCE Center. Former Chair of DBMI. Professor of Medicine. Research on biomedical and clinical research informatics, learning health systems, and translational informatics.

faculty.name: Adam Wright
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Director of Vanderbilt Clinical Informatics Center (VCLIC). Professor of Biomedical Informatics and Medicine. Research on EHRs, clinical decision support, detecting CDS malfunctions using ML, and predicting adverse events. Author of over 200 peer-reviewed publications.

faculty.name: Paul Harris
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Professor of Biomedical Informatics, Biomedical Engineering, and Biostatistics. Creator of REDCap platform serving nearly 7900 institutional partners worldwide. Founder of ResearchMatch national volunteer registry. Leads All of Us Protocol and Data & Research Center at VUMC.

faculty.name: Michael E. Matheny
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.sub_field: Predictive Analytics and Medical Device Surveillance
faculty.summary: Director of CIPHI. Professor of Biomedical Informatics, Medicine, and Biostatistics. Research on predictive analytics, machine learning, data mining, and NLP for medical device safety surveillance and health services research in diabetes and AKI.

faculty.name: Wei-Qi Wei
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Associate Professor of Biomedical Informatics. Director of Precision Phenotyping Core. Research on informatics tools for phenotyping through terminology, NLP, and ML. Author of 200+ publications and creator of PheMAP, Phecode, and MEDI tools.

faculty.name: Colin G. Walsh
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.sub_field: Predictive Analytics and Mental Health Informatics
faculty.summary: Associate Professor of Biomedical Informatics, Medicine, and Psychiatry. Research on predictive analytics for vulnerable populations, ML for mental health and suicide prevention, point-of-care decision support, and value-based healthcare.

faculty.name: Daniel Fabbri
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.sub_field: Healthcare Data Management and Machine Learning
faculty.summary: Associate Professor of Biomedical Informatics and Computer Science. Director of Informatics Innovation at VUMC. Research on data management and machine learning applied to EMRs and clinical data. Founded Maize Analytics for medical record access auditing (acquired 2021).

faculty.name: Kim M. Unertl
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Director of Graduate Studies. Associate Professor of Biomedical Informatics. Research on clinical workflow and health IT fit, IT design and implementation strategies, and broadening the biomedical informatics pipeline.

faculty.name: You Chen
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.sub_field: AI and Network Analysis in Healthcare
faculty.summary: Associate Professor of Biomedical Informatics. Director of OHPENLab. Research on graph-based machine learning and AI methods for healthcare, including EHR audit log analysis, drug safety monitoring, care team modeling, and health equity. Recipient of Presidential Early Career Award (PECASE).

faculty.name: Thomas Lasko
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.sub_field: Medical Machine Learning and Precision Medicine
faculty.summary: Associate Professor of Biomedical Informatics and Computer Science. Research on unsupervised machine learning for clinical data, discovering disease subtypes from EMR data, and computational aspects of precision medicine. Previously at Google developing Symptom Search algorithm.

faculty.name: Allison McCoy
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Associate Professor of Biomedical Informatics. Director of Clinical Informatics Core. Research on EHRs, clinical decision support implementation, optimization and evaluation, and improving EHR data access for research.

faculty.name: Laurie L. Novak
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Associate Professor of Biomedical Informatics. Research on qualitative approaches to information systems and clinical work, social construction of risk and safety, and clinician strategies for maintaining safety during IT implementation.

faculty.name: Zhijun Yin
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Assistant Professor of Biomedical Informatics. Research on data mining and machine learning for modeling health behaviors and outcomes in online environments including social media, health communities, and patient portals.

faculty.name: Chao Yan
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Assistant Professor of Biomedical Informatics. Research on synthetic health data generation and evaluation, predictive modeling, and privacy protection in health data use.

faculty.name: Siru Liu
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.sub_field: EHR Optimization and Large Language Models
faculty.summary: Assistant Professor of Biomedical Informatics. Research on optimizing EHR features and functions, especially clinical decision support, leveraging ML and LLMs for healthcare quality, and analyzing public perceptions via social and behavioral science.

faculty.name: Roch Guérin
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Harold B. and Adelaide G. Welge Professor and Department Chair. Research focuses on computer networks and communication systems, with contributions to quality of service, traffic engineering, and network architecture.

faculty.name: Kunal Agrawal
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research on parallel computing, real-time and cyber-physical systems, with emphasis on scheduling theory and deterministic parallelism.

faculty.name: Sanjoy Baruah
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Leading researcher in real-time and safety-critical systems, scheduling theory, and mixed-criticality system design. Major contributions to the theory and practice of real-time scheduling.

faculty.name: Aaron Bobick
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Dean of McKelvey School of Engineering and James M. McKelvey Professor. Research in computer vision, human-robot interaction, and perceptual computing.

faculty.name: Ian Bogost
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research at the intersection of computational media studies, game design, and platform studies. Prominent voice in video game criticism and computational media theory.

faculty.name: Michael Brent
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Henry Edwin Sever Professor. Research in computational biology and genomics, developing algorithms and machine learning methods for understanding gene regulation and biological systems.

faculty.name: Jeremy Buhler
faculty.title: Professor

faculty.title: Professor

faculty.name: Yixin Chen
faculty.title: Professor

faculty.name: Patrick Crowley
faculty.title: Professor

faculty.name: Ron Cytron
faculty.title: Professor

faculty.name: Christopher Gill
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in parallel and distributed real-time embedded systems, with focus on middleware, scheduling, and cyber-physical system design.

faculty.name: Nathan Jacobs
faculty.title: Professor

faculty.title: Professor

faculty.title: Professor

faculty.title: Professor

faculty.name: Holly Rushmeier
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Department Chair. Research in computer graphics, focusing on data acquisition for realistic rendering, 3D scanning, and computational tools for cultural heritage preservation.

faculty.name: James Aspnes
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Harold W. Cheel Professor. Research in distributed algorithms, population protocols, and fault-tolerant computing. Received the 2020 ACM-EATCS Dijkstra Prize in Distributed Computing for foundational work on computation in passively mobile sensor networks.

faculty.name: Abhishek Bhattacharjee
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: A. Bartlett Giamatti Professor. Research in computer architecture, with focus on memory management, virtualization, and hardware-software interfaces for emerging computing platforms.

faculty.name: Yang Cai
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Co-Director of the Center for Algorithms, Data, and Market Design (CADMY). Research in algorithmic game theory, mechanism design, multi-agent learning, and the interface of computation and economics. FOCS 2022 Test of Time Award.

faculty.name: Julie Dorsey
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in computer graphics with emphasis on realistic image synthesis, architectural and urban modeling, and visual perception.

faculty.name: Joan Feigenbaum
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Grace Murray Hopper Professor. Research in security, privacy, and trust management, with foundational contributions to the algorithmic and systems foundations of internet security.

faculty.name: Michael Fischer
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Foundational researcher in distributed computing and cryptography. Known for the FLP impossibility result (Fischer-Lynch-Paterson) establishing fundamental limits of distributed consensus.

faculty.name: David Gelernter
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Pioneer in parallel computing and software architecture. Known for the Linda coordination language and tuple spaces model for parallel programming.

faculty.name: Theodore Kim
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in computer graphics with focus on physics-based animation and simulation, developing numerical methods for visual effects and computational physics.

faculty.name: Ruzica Piskac
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in formal methods with focus on automated verification, program synthesis, and software correctness. Develops techniques for building provably correct software systems.

faculty.name: Brian Scassellati
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in socially assistive robotics and human-robot interaction, developing robots that collaborate with people in educational and therapeutic settings.

faculty.name: Zhong Shao
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in programming languages and formal methods, focusing on certified software, verified compilation, and building provably secure operating systems.

faculty.name: Avi Silberschatz
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Renowned researcher in database systems and operating systems. Co-author of influential textbooks on operating systems and database systems used worldwide.

faculty.name: Daniel Spielman
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Sterling Professor (joint with Mathematics). Leading researcher in theoretical computer science. Known for smoothed analysis of algorithms, spectral graph theory, and fast Laplacian solvers. MacArthur Fellow and member of both National Academy of Sciences and National Academy of Engineering.

faculty.name: Vladimir Rokhlin
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Professor of Computer Science and Mathematics. Co-inventor of the fast multipole method, one of the top ten algorithms of the 20th century. Research in numerical analysis and scientific computing.

faculty.name: Nisheeth Vishnoi
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: A. Bartlett Giamatti Professor. Research in algorithms with emphasis on the theoretical foundations of machine learning, optimization, and the societal impact of artificial intelligence and computing.

faculty.name: Y. Richard Yang
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in computer networks with focus on software-defined networking, network optimization, and mobile network architectures. Co-founder of multiple networking companies.

faculty.name: Lin Zhong
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Joseph C. Tsai Professor and Director of Graduate Studies. Research in mobile and energy-efficient computing systems, operating systems, and human-computer interaction.

faculty.name: Steven Zucker
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: David M. Boies Professor (joint with Biomedical Engineering). Research in computer vision and computational neuroscience, studying the mathematical foundations of visual perception.

faculty.name: Charalampos Papamanthou
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research in cryptography and security, with focus on verifiable computation, authenticated data structures, and privacy-preserving cloud computing.

faculty.name: Robert Soulé
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research in computer systems and networking with focus on programmable network data planes, distributed systems, and high-performance networked systems.

faculty.name: Arman Cohan
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research in natural language processing with focus on large language models, scientific text understanding, and information extraction from scholarly literature.

faculty.name: Yongshan Ding
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research in quantum computing with focus on quantum computer architecture, quantum software systems, and bridging classical and quantum computation.

faculty.name: Benjamin Fisch
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research in cryptography with focus on proof systems, verifiable computing, and the security foundations of blockchain and decentralized systems.

faculty.name: Tesca Fitzgerald

faculty.summary: Research in robotics with focus on interactive learning and human-robot collaboration, developing robots that learn from and adapt to human partners.

faculty.name: Quanquan Liu
